text: A fun movie that at times is very violent/gory but with great action sequences. Sometimes the plot veers off its track but it's still a fun waste of time.

These little lights are way smaller than they look in the pictures. The magnet barely holds onto anything, keeps sliding right off my fridge. Main light is okay, but the side flashlight is pretty weak. Also, the clip feels super cheap and snapped the first time I tried to hang one from my belt loop. Ended up tossing two in a junk drawer and one in the glovebox. Honestly, not worth the hassle for any real workshop use.

It's an okay little heater, but honestly I expected a bit more warmth for a pad this size. Installation was simple, but I didn't realize it would block so much of the glass once it's stuck on, feels a bit permanent. It does work as advertised, just not the best fit for my setup. Overall, it's functional but underwhelming for the price.

I grabbed these for a backyard movie night and honestly, for what I paid, I'm impressed. The remote range is a little spotty if you're too far away, but once you get the hang of it, the lights add a nice glow. Battery life held up all evening too. Perfect for casual decorating without spending much.

I've been using this hoof supplement for about four months now, and I'm cautiously optimistic. My gelding has always had brittle, shelly feet that chipped if you looked at them wrong. After the first tub, I didn't see much change, but I stuck with it. Around the two-month mark, I noticed the new growth coming in tighter and smoother. His farrier even commented last trim that the nail holes were holding better and there was less crumbling. He's still not barefoot-ready over gravel, but the durability improvement is real. One star off because I wish the pellets were a little more palatable, he leaves them sometimes if I don't mix with grain. Overall, a solid option.

I love this whole series and Ward's writing. This one has left me hanging more than usual. Is Jo going to be a vamp/who will she love/how is this going to bring in the reporter boss? How is Tohr going to find out about his brother and will he end up being a brother? So many many more questions. I will be waiting impatiently got the next (with a bit of hope that some of this will be answered in the newer Black Dagger Series).

I bought 3 of these - One small, one large, and one XL. The large and XL fit my husky and Pit bull perfectly, but the small one, for my small 12 lb bichon mix was way too tight. Maybe this is my misinterpretation, however, I think most folks would think a Small would fit a 12 lb dog w/ a small head and neck. Other than the fact that I think it is mislabeled, (and the fact that the jingling bells will drive anyone crazy after a few minutes), I would give this item 5 stars. It is very festive, holiday-appropriate, well-made, elastic, and the dogs seemed to peacefully accept wearing it after only a minute or so.
Great story... William Hurt is amazing, and it's def. a movie every doctor should watch. I love movies where the character is forced to re-examine their beliefs.

I'm honestly impressed before I even put it on. The top arrived two days early in a slim, recyclable mailer, no weird plastic smell, no creases from being crammed into a box. It was folded neatly with a little tissue paper, which felt surprisingly thoughtful for an activewear piece. First touch: the fabric is silky-cool and substantial, not flimsy at all. I ordered the teal, and the color is exactly like the listing photos, deep and saturated. I'm a 34D, usually between medium and large, and the large drapes perfectly without being baggy. The reversible neckline is such a clever touch; it instantly gives you two different looks. Immediately ordered a second one.

I wasn't expecting much for the price, but the magnetic pieces are the real star here, my 4-year-old can actually connect them without getting frustrated and screaming for help every two minutes. They snap together solidly but aren't too stiff for little hands, so she builds these wild, lopsided castles all by herself. It's been on our coffee table for months now and still looks new despite daily abuse. I also appreciate that the magnets are fully enclosed, so I'm not constantly worried about one popping out. Great open-ended toy that keeps her busy while I cook dinner, and cleanup is just tossing everything back in the box.

Very nice knife, I have three Havalon knives and they are all quality in my opinion. They also have multiple uses not just skinning and filleting. I use them more in the kitchen then in the field. The replaceable blades are so convenient to use and easy to replace and I have found they last a long time if they are used for other purposes like cutting veggies and making nice cuts in chicken for stuffed chicken breast. For the price and the quality you can't go wrong. Same review as the Baracuta Fillet both excellent.

This is perfect! It will stack in any order, it will spin as if they're tops, they will roll quite a distance on their sides as if they're wheels, my infants enjoy them and so does my 7 year old child.

They are very well made, comfortable, and warm. One of the best fitting pair of socks I've ever owned. They also do a great job of wicking the moisture away from your skin. I highly recommend them to anyone looking for a great quality sock.
This is a great light for the price. Seriously it far exceeds my expectations and I happily recommend it to all who ask about it when out for a ride.
Bottle arrived with a loose cap and polish leaked all over the bubble mailer. First impression was a sticky, dented mess before I even got to use it.
Cute idea but after a month the pieces are already peeling and looking beat up.

Each of them are running from their pastor history. Alpha son/street muscian, daughter of wanna be an alpha's (any alpha's) mate mother meet. The attraction is there. Can they support each other and realize that the history they are running from doesn't have to be theirs? Facing up to grow up may just be what they really want.
Light's still fine after a few months of riding, but the mount cracked at the pivot after some bumpy roads. Held together with tape now. Okay for the price, just wish it lasted longer.

The deep-space combat scenes and hard-science world-building are solid, exactly what I wanted. But the pacing drags whenever the crew debates ancient alien philosophy for pages on end. It stalls the momentum I loved in earlier chapters. Still worth a read for the tactical action alone, just be ready to skim the talky bits.

I wasted an evening trying to flash a basic PIC16F chip and never got past the device-not-found errors. The drivers installed fine, but the programmer refuses to talk to anything. I honestly can't recommend this to anyone who values their time, unless you enjoy troubleshooting badly cloned hardware with zero documentation. Maybe it's okay for someone who just wants a paperweight shaped like a programmer, but if you actually need to program microcontrollers, spend the extra on a genuine Pickit.

I paid actual money for this, which stings more than the wooden dialogue. Even at a discount rental price it's overpriced. I've seen student films with more believable reactions. Total waste of a quiet evening and my bandwidth.

The box showed up pretty banged up, but everything inside was okay. Gear looked decent out of the package, just hoping it holds up. Shipping took a day longer than expected. Not blown away, not disappointed either.
Unfortunately the articulation makes the figure's top half look a little freakish. But If that doesn't bother you, you get a lot of playability and displayability with that articulation.
Loved the witty dialogue and snappy pacing, but the ending felt a bit rushed for how carefully the mystery was set up.

Lifeboat (Alfred Hitchcock, 1944) You take a John Steinbeck story, have Jo Swerling (Pennies from Heaven) adapt it, and get Alfred Hitchcock to direct, with a cast including Tallulah Bankhead, William Bendix, Hume Cronyn, Henry Hull, and Walter Slezak, among others. How can you not have a hit? Lifeboat is a simple tale: a ship has gone down, torpedoed by a German U-Boat that was destroyed soon after. A number of survivors struggle to a lifeboat, one of whom is a crewman from the U-Boat. Second-guessing, paranoia, and power struggles ensue. The best thing to do: get out of the way. And that's pretty much what Hitchcock does; each of these characters is invested with a distinct, well-drawn personality, and given the situation, they're bound to clash on their own. Change a camera angle here and there, perhaps, but let the characters do their thing. There's a minimal plot (regarding, of course, the eeeeeeeeevil German-- let's remember this film was released during the darkest days of World War II), but this is a movie that's really about these characters and how they interact with one another. And it's fascinating.

I honestly didn't think it would live up to the hype, but it's even smoother than I imagined. The ergonomics feel dialed in, and the blade centering was perfect right out of the box. It's become my go-to for everyday carry. Zero regrets spending a bit more, this thing just feels right.

I grabbed this on sale and honestly wasn't expecting much given the price, but it's been a gem. Autofocus locks on instantly, even with my dog darting around the yard, and the burst shooting lets me actually catch the moment instead of missing it by a second. Battery life has been solid for long day trips too. For what I paid, the image quality feels like a steal, colors pop without much tweaking. It's made photography genuinely fun again without breaking the bank.

I held off on this knife forever because I wasn't sure about the deployment without a flipper, but it's genuinely smooth once you get the motion down. The action is glassy and locks up with zero play in any direction. The blade came wicked sharp out of the box and has held its edge through a bunch of cardboard breakdowns and camping meal prep. Ergonomics are spot on for my medium hands, and the handle texture gives great grip even when things get wet. It carries surprisingly light in the pocket for its size, too. For what I paid, the materials and build quality feel like a serious step above anything else in this range. Easily my new go-to everyday carry.
Went in with low expectations and was completely surprised. It's one of those rare films that actually gets better with every rewatch.

I'll be honest, I didn't expect much at this price point, figured I'd get something rough around the edges that needed a ton of tuning. Nope. Right out of the box, the sole was flat, the blade was surprisingly sharp, and I was shaving whisper-thin curls off some scrap oak within five minutes. The adjustment controls feel solid, not sloppy, which makes dialing in depth super quick. I've used it mostly for chamfering edges and flushing up joints on some cabinet doors I'm building, and it's saved me a bunch of sanding. For the money, this little thing punches way above its weight. If you're on the fence, just grab one, you'll find excuses to use it constantly.

Got for my 5 year old son and Georgie couldn't hear or respond to him. My husband and I had to do it, and it couldn't always underdtand us which was frustrating. The quality of it is good, and it's very cute, just ended up not working out for us.

I was intrigued by this, having seen a part of an episode on tv. It starts off great, interesting characters, good plot but then becomes silly and the end of Series One is so ridiculous that I threw the box in the garbage!
I've been using these for a few months now on weekend projects and they've held up great. No bending or wear yet, even with heavy use. Solid metal, honestly can't beat them for the price.

I was hoping for a page-turner based on all the buzz, but this fell completely flat. The story never let me settle into it, every single character felt like a cardboard cutout with a label slapped on, telling me exactly what to think instead of showing anything. There's no room to breathe or form your own impressions, which kills any sense of discovery. The dialogue, especially during what should've been a tense confrontation, felt stilted and weightless, like the author was just moving pieces around. I've read debut novels with more depth and subtlety. Honestly, by the halfway point I was just skimming, waiting for something to grab me, and it never did. Really disappointing compared to what the description promised.
Did not correctly fit my 20" screen. If it fit I would have kept it.
Just as good as the battery that comes with the GoPro but cheaper and you get two. I have not tried to charge the official battery in the charger but the Wasabi batteries charge in the GoPro fine.
This clock is large, easy to read, inexpensive, and gets the job done.
David did a pretty good job but he is no SL.

I picked this up for my niece's 7th birthday and she's been completely hooked. It's a warm, gentle set that actually holds her attention. Perfect for any parent or grandparent wanting screen time that builds character without being preachy. I'd hand this to anyone with little kids.

Easy to assemble. Took me only a few minutes to put it all together. Comes with instructions and a small wrench. I weight 180lbs and it holds up very well using all variations of grip. Only advice, use tape or towel to wrap up parts that presses against the wall to prevent marks and scratches and reinforce the some what weak black foam that it comes with. Other than that, everything works as advertise. Just put it to work and be consistences, a healthy diet then everything else will follow. =) Live Happy Heathy and Thrive

I've tried way pricier pants that didn't fit this well. For what they cost, the quality is honestly surprising, sturdy stitching and the material feels great. Sized down one per the chart and they're spot on. Definitely grabbing another pair.

My son is 3 and he absolutely loved these puzzles! He plays with it for at least 20 minutes at a time, dumping all of the pieces together and then putting each puzzle together separately. He rarely even needs to ask for help. As a parent I LOVE the different shapes printed on the back of the pieces to differentiate the separate puzzles when putting them away in their own compartments. I highly recommend it as a great toy for any child 3 and up.

What woman wouldn't want five gorgeous, intelligent men focused strictly on her pleasure? We should all be so lucky! These characters are so well written you start falling in love with them immediately. This story is sensuous, sexy and erotic. Savannah grew up under the rule of an emotionless, iron fisted father. No wonder the woman is still a virgin at 30 something. Matt is dominant without being domineering. He's shown her in a hundred different ways how he loves her. How did she miss the signs? Looking forward to read each of the Wunderkind stories. Such unique personalities that we've only been given a glimpse of.
Perfect for anyone wanting reliable, long-lasting earbuds for daily use.
Used these on a dozen ice fishing trips this season and they still kick out steady heat for hours. No signs of wearing out.

I've been on a mission to declutter my sewing corner, and this bin is perfect for holding all my spools, measuring tape, and those tiny scissors that always vanish. The clear sides make it easy to spot exactly what I need without digging around, and the stackable design means I'm finally using vertical space on my shelf instead of piling things on top of each other. It feels sturdy but light enough to grab with one hand. I'd absolutely recommend this to anyone with a craft stash, a junk drawer that haunts them, or even a kid's art supply mess, it brings a little peace to the chaos without looking like industrial storage.

I've read this three times now and the spine's still tight, no loose pages, no fading print. For a paperback I've tossed in backpacks and spilled coffee on, it's held up shockingly well. The action hits hard every reread, and the cover barely shows wear. Solid build for a book that actually gets used.

I picked this up hoping it'd blend in better with our living room than those clunky wire kennels, and looks-wise it definitely delivers. The fabric's nice and the zipper glides smoothly, but the frame isn't as sturdy as I expected for the price. My 40-pound pup leaned against one side and I could see the bars flex a bit. It works fine for calm indoor use, just don't count on it for anything beyond that.

This plush looked cool when I saw it online but once I had it in hand then I did realize that it was even better in person. This thing is amazing! I just cant say enough nice things about it. Its made of top quality materials, it's soft, and it's so ugly its cute! On top of all that, 10% of the purchase proceedings goes toward saving this endangered animal. Now you can't get more noble then that. Get yourself one of these! It's too cool.
This one caught me off guard in the best way. For the rental price, the chaotic energy and sharp comedic timing made it an easy, fun watch.
My four year old LOVES this camera. He received it as a Christmas gift and its still going strong.
Order arrived a day early in perfect, sturdy packaging. Everything inside was sealed and pristine. My nephew ripped it open and started shuffling before I even finished my coffee, instant hit.
I expected decent sneakers, but these blew me away, feels like I'm walking on clouds. The airy fabric keeps my feet cool on long strolls. Honestly the best buy I've made this year.

Great paddle! Very light-weight and sturdy. It is much more easy to use than the paddle that came with my paddle board. It is also easy to adjust or take apart and even came with a great carrying case.

I got this cube on a whim and it's become my go-to fidget toy. The turning is smooth right out of the box, no catching or stiffness, which makes solving it super satisfying. That hollow center is such a clever design, I'll slide it onto my thumb while carrying groceries or my coffee mug, and it just hangs there securely. I've been using a simple repetitive sequence to cycle it back to solved, and it's oddly meditative. The matte finish feels nice and doesn't get slippery. Honestly, it's a small thing that's added a lot of calm to my busy days. Great quality for the price.

This Lucky Stars flick is pure chaos in the best way. The chemistry between the cast is electric, and the fight choreography had me rewinding scenes just to catch every stunt. Genuine laughs mixed with that classic Hong Kong action energy. A total blast from start to finish.

I honestly expected this to be shredded by now, but it's still totally intact after weeks. My heavy chewer goes nuts for it every single day. We just stuff it with a little peanut butter and it keeps him busy forever. Best tough toy we've bought.
I usually spend way more on incense, but these cedarwood sticks smell incredible for the price.
Got this scarf for a steal and it looks way pricier.

My book arrived a full day early and was packed so securely, no bent corners or scuffs at all, which honestly surprised me. The cover had this gorgeous matte finish that felt great right out of the box, and I flipped through immediately. The banter between Colin and Della hooked me from chapter one. It's been ages since a historical romance made me laugh and swoon this fast. Totally worth the preorder.

The product arrived quickly. I machine washed it and was afraid the tassles for the bottom would fall off or tangle buy they are fine! The inner pads can be easily removed and I would definitely agree with buying at least a size up. In all, a very cute and affordable bikini! I have received this product at a discounted rate for an honest review.

I grabbed one of these on a whim before a weekend camping trip and it ended up being the star of the whole outing. Walking trails after dark felt totally safe because the beam reaches way out and lights up everything evenly, not just a tiny hot spot in the middle. Battery life held up through three nights of pretty constant use without dimming at all. The size is just right, slips into a jacket pocket or clips onto a hat brim without weighing you down. It survived getting dropped on rocks and dunked in a stream, still works like day one. Honestly, I've paid double for lights that didn't perform this well. Solid piece of gear.
Big, sturdy, comfortable. No clue what the long skinny pocket on the inside would be for, but it doesn't take away from the bag.

I bought these mainly for mandoline slicing after a nasty mishap left me missing a chunk of thumb, and they've been an absolute game changer. They fit snug without feeling restrictive, and I can still easily grip slippery veggies or awkwardly shaped cheeses. The first few times I tested them, I nervously ran a blade over my palm, zero damage, not even a scratch. I've since used them while shucking oysters and handling my microplane without any drama. They wash up easily and dry fast, so I actually use them instead of leaving them in a drawer. Honestly, the peace of mind alone makes them worth every penny.
As described. Fish seem to enjoy swimming against the current created.
Feels cheap for what it cost, barely does the one job I needed.
The pacing was solid, but the third act felt rushed and some character decisions didn't make sense.
great headphones. sounds good and loud as I like it. a must buy!!! the headphones comes with three different size rubbers and a case for storage very nice.

It works very well for smoothing my cat's coat. It does not remove tough knots but I have a very fine tooth comb for that. This is a finishing brush. I like that the metal bristles retract, making it easy to clean.
Good quality at a reasonable price. Love not having to put metal tags on the collars yet still having the important info on it.

We have had several Lamaze toys and this one was the favorite with all three of our children. I have found that babies love the crinkling noises best and these have very large wings that are easy to manipulate to make the sound. My kids all loved the ladybug teether and the bright colors as well. This has a large strong clip that stays securely attached to the stroller, car seat and diaper bag. They have pulled so hard on this toy many times, putting their full weight on it and it doesn't stretch open and pop off like other clip-on toys have. I have thrown ours in the washing machine on gentle a few times and it has held up beautifully.

The knife lives in the door pocket of my car. I've used it a few times. I give it a 4 because the fire stick is not very good compared to others I've purchased. Otherwise, it is simply what you expect.
Used this to spray over a little tikes slide. Went on great. Used whole can so recommend getting two if you are doing similar projects

I am a big Beatles fan but always had mixed feelings about John. He was obviously a great musician and showman. His Imagine album is wonderful, and his plea for peace in the title song is downright anthemic for the peace movement. He truly believed in love; his favorite lyric was "All you need is love." At the same time, for much of his life he was often nastily sarcastic, hotheaded, and abusive, especially when drinking. My understanding of his boyhood before I saw this film was that Lennon liked to exaggerate how difficult his situation had been, separated from his mother and raised by his Aunt Mimi, when in reality he had a very comfortable, secure, middle-class upbringing. I continued to hold this view throughout the film, thinking how good he had it: a reunion with a mother who was fun, doting, and indulgent while being watched over by a strict, but loving, aunt. That is, until the moment when Mimi revealed to him exactly the circumstances of the loss of his father and separation from his mother. Then I got it. And when he finally lost his mother for good, I can see why he described his growing up years as hard. I have much more sympathy for him now.
Pages are still crisp after multiple rereads, no spine creases.

I switched to this after my ferret started sneezing with paper bedding, and it's been a game changer. Zero dust, practically no odor even after a full week, and cleanup is way easier. Cage stays fresh, and my little guy burrows in it constantly.

This was a quick, fun read with just the right amount of drama. I liked the messy, tangled relationships and how everything spiraled once the scheming started. The characters felt real, even when they made frustrating choices. My only small gripe is I wished the ending had a bit more breathing room. Solid entertainment overall.

Arrived way faster than expected and the packaging was seriously overbuilt, each weight wrapped tight with zero scuffs out of the box. First thing I noticed was the clean coating and how solid they feel in hand. Already put them through a few sessions and they're holding up beautifully. Super happy with this set.
I was worried they'd feel bulky, but the dainty fit makes them perfect for everyday wear.
I wasn't expecting such thick, soft material for under forty bucks, really pulls my reading nook together. Feels way more expensive than it cost.

For the price, I expected a bit more. The balls fit loose in the tray, and the wood feels really cheap, like it'll chip after a few uses. It works, but honestly I'd rather have spent a few extra bucks on a sturdier set. Kinda disappointed.

When Chad Douglas agreed to help Cassandra Davidson take her show horses to an event, he didn't know what he was getting intoand neither did she. He was merely doing a favor for a friend but ended up trying to tame the spoiled little rich girl who, however, stirred powerful emotions within him. Raised by wealthy, uncaring parents, Cassandra didn't know what discipline means, but she found out with Chad, and to her surprise, she liked it. Sexual tension mingles with an interesting storyline, well-developed characters, and beautiful descriptions.

I had fun with Bad Teacher the first time around, Cameron Diaz really sells the whole trainwreck vibe, and Jason Segel's dry humor landed for me. But on a second watch a few months later, the jokes didn't hold up nearly as well. It's the kind of comedy that feels sharp once and then gets pretty thin. A lot of the shock-value scenes just felt forced when I already knew the punchlines. I'd say it's worth a rental for a lazy night, but don't expect it to stay fresh in your collection. It wore out its welcome faster than I expected, which is why I'm landing on three stars.
Perfect for anyone who always loses their 10mm, the holder keeps things tidy.

I bought this set for the guest bedroom after hunting for something that wouldn't break the bank but still felt decent. The fabric is soft right out of the package and has gotten even cozier after a couple washes. I appreciate that it's pure cotton, it breathes well and doesn't trap heat, which is a must in our climate. The faint pattern adds just enough character without clashing with other decor. I did notice a few loose threads around the button closure, but nothing that affected function. Overall, it's a solid find that balances cost and comfort nicely, and my guests have commented on how inviting the bed looks.
The plot kept me hooked, and the cast really delivered. Some scenes were more intense than I expected, but it didn't ruin the ride. A solid watch overall with great pacing and a satisfying finish.

Received a copy of Perfectly Kissed in exchange for an honest review. This book was a great short story. The characters have chemistry from the start but they also have a history. I don't want to give to much away but lets just say you get a cute kid, a cranky old lady who knows what she's talking about and sexy man or two. I loved it and can't wait to read more!

I grabbed one of these on a whim because the price was too good to ignore, and it's become my go-to pocket companion. It's so unobtrusive that I genuinely forget it's clipped to my keychain until I need it, which happens more often than I expected. Opening packages, tightening a loose screw on a cabinet hinge, prying open a stuck battery cover, it handles all those little daily annoyances without a fuss. The build quality surprised me; it feels solid despite being featherlight. I work in an office where pulling out anything that looks remotely like a blade would get side-eye, but this little thing blends right in. Honestly, it's saved me so many trips to the supply closet. Solid purchase.
I usually feed my dog the dry version of this. Every few days I give her this for dinner and She loves it.
IT IS THE BEST, VERY EASY TO UNDERSTAND,QUICK SHIPPING. WITH BALLET IT HELPS TO WATCH AGAIN AND AGAIN TO MAKE SURE OF YOUR PREFORMANCE IS ABOVE ALL SUCCESSFUL.WOULD DEFINATELY BUY AGAIN.
Beautiful slippers! Very cute and stylish. A great gift, from a great designer. I will enjoy giving them this Christmas!
Finally something that actually keeps the pad put no matter how wild my little guy gets. Makes cleanup so much less of a headache.

For ten bucks it's fine. The clip is stiff and tricky one-handed, but it's compact and does the job on short walks. Not fancy, just decent value if you want something small and don't mind wrestling the carabiner.

I went in with pretty high hopes since Rachael Johns rarely disappoints, and this one mostly delivered. The emotional weight of the missing child case hooked me right away, and the way the fallout ripples through everyone involved felt authentic rather than melodramatic. I especially liked the dynamic with the new characters who shake things up. A couple of plot beats felt a tad predictable in the back half, which kept it from being a total knockout, but the Aussie setting and genuine heart still made it a solid, satisfying read.
My cat finally stopped scratching the sofa after just three days.

Pros. 1. Feels like 'quality'. has some heft to it. gives you a warm, fuzzy when you first take it out of the box. 2. Lots of accessories. everything you need to get started vice a SD card is in the box. including a short HDMI cable. Cons. 1. Terrible video quality. just terrible. grainy and washed out. you will not be able to read a license plate of a car parked just in front and to the left or right of you. 2. Operation is not intuitive. crazyest sequence of button pushes I've ever experienced. the 'OK' button is not always 'Ok'. button pushing is on par with entering a cheat code in a video game console. I. e. UP, DOWN, MODE, UP, OK, MENU, MODE, UP, DOWN, UP, UP. argghh. 3. Freezes constantly. tape a paper clip to the side of it as your going to need to reset it constantly. 4. Received a used or open box unit. everything was there, but there were fingerprints all over the lense, the instruction manual was all bent to crap, the protective film and zip lock bags the originally held the pieces where all at the bottom of the box. This was returned (and would have been anyway since it was pre-owned/used). I have ordered a main-stream GARMIN to take its place.

I grabbed my usual size 10 first and my toes were jammed against the front, definitely go up a half size, maybe a full size if you're between. Once I swapped for a 10.5, it was night and day. These sneakers have become my everyday staple. I've walked miles in them on city trips and my feet felt fresh the whole time. The cushioning is plush without being mushy, and the styling works with everything from joggers to chinos. Already eyeing another pair.

I bought this doll for my 16 month old daughter who showed interest in giving her pacifier to another doll, so I figured we'd try this one out. She seems to like 'feeding' the baby and watching her go to 'sleep' and we both like the fact that this doll has a feature that closes/opens the eyes depending on which way the bottle is 'fed'. So far this doll has been a hit; it's soft body is fun to cuddle and carry, the eyes are cute and it's a good size, not too bulky. We give this one an A+++
Arrived super fast in minimal packaging. Opened it up and immediately noticed the material feels premium and breathable. Really promising first impression.

I really wanted to like this, especially for what I paid, but it just doesn't hold up. The concept is charming enough on paper, but the execution feels amateurish. You can see some of the stage director's touch in the blocking, but it never translates to film, everything feels stiff and weirdly theatrical in a bad way. For the price of a rental, maybe, but as a purchase it's not worth the shelf space. I've gotten more entertainment from bargain bin finds. The pacing drags, the leads don't click, and I was checking my phone halfway through. Save your money for something with actual rewatch value.
Bought as a gift. The laughs alone were worth the money. Not sure if it works...
this holster is a great paddle holster. puts the gun in a good position and holds it snug. I would buy it again.
my 3 yr old loves giving her baby a bath in this adorable doll tub

The box arrived in perfect shape, no dings or bent corners, which is always a relief when you're picky about your books. It was wrapped snug with that heavy kraft paper, not just tossed in loose. Right out of the package, the cover felt smooth and substantial, with a matte finish that doesn't collect fingerprints. Flipping through, the pages are thick enough that there's no bleed-through, and the print is crisp and easy on the eyes. I cracked it open expecting dense clinical language, but even the introduction felt like a calm, wise conversation. You can tell real care went into the physical book itself, it's the kind you'll want to keep on the nightstand, not shove on a shelf. Really pleased with the whole unboxing experience.
While these are a little pricey, my cat asks for one everyday. And they are good for her teeth, so well worth the purchase
Cuter than expected, but the fabric's a bit thinner than I hoped.

I'm really happy with the sleek matte black finish, it matches my door hardware perfectly and feels sturdy. The fingerprint reader is quick most of the time, though it occasionally takes a second scan to register. Installation was straightforward, even for a DIY newbie like me. Overall, a solid upgrade that's given me peace of mind, just wish the sensor was a tad more consistent.

Bought this for a date night and the lace detail is even prettier in person. It's soft, fits true to size, and made me feel super confident without being uncomfortable. Definitely a keeper for special occasions.

I don't see how we've been able to watch this as many times as we have since purchasing it. But honestly, it's entertaining as heck and doesn't make me want to rupture my ear drums when my toddler wants to watch it every single day.

We've been using this thing for a little over ten months now, and honestly, it's been a game changer for our backyard routine. Setup was straightforward and our dog took to it right away with no hesitation. I was a little skeptical about how it'd hold up with a bigger pup, but it's been solid with zero messes or smells escaping. The maintenance is basically nonexistent, just a quick scoop and rinse now and then. Our yard stays clean, and we don't have to do the daily poop hunt anymore. If you follow the install instructions and actually dig the hole deep enough, it works like a charm. Wish we'd gotten one sooner.
Wore these a ton for months and they still look brand new.

For a Kindle deal, this was an absolute steal. The plot moves at a nice clip and the world-building feels surprisingly rich without being bloated. I got the whole series after finishing this because the per-book cost was so reasonable. A few pacing lulls kept it from being perfect, but for the price, it's a solid escape.
These figures were way more detailed than I expected, feels like they jumped straight out of the cartoon. My kid hasn't put them down.
Great for fans of twisty thrillers who don't need a perfect ending.

I grabbed this on a whim when it was discounted, and honestly, it delivered way more than I expected for the price. The pacing keeps things moving fast, and the chemistry between leads is genuinely steamy without feeling forced. You get real closure on lingering questions from the first book, plus enough tension to keep you flipping pages. For what I paid, the sheer entertainment value here is a steal, perfect weekend escape read.
I'm sure it's fine but bird will not touch it

Bought this canvas duffel last fall to stash a bulky 9-foot artificial tree (four sections, all wired with lights). After wrestling it in and out of the attic twice now, I'm honestly surprised how well it's held up. The stitching around the handles hasn't loosened at all, and I've dragged it across some rough plywood floors without a single tear. Zipper still runs smooth even when the bag is crammed full. I was half expecting the fabric to fray by the second season, but it looks practically new. For the price, this thing is a beast, way tougher than those flimsy storage bags that split at the seams. Definitely a solid buy if you need something that lasts.

Arrived a day early in a simple plastic bag, no damage. First thing I noticed, these are way thicker than expected, almost overbuilt for small desk cables. The velcro grabs instantly and holds tight. A little stiff to wrap at first, but after a few uses they soften up. Nice having so many in one pack; I've already organized three drawers.

I've been using this router for a few months now and it's held up better than I expected for the price. I'd say it's a solid pick for someone who just needs basic, no-fuss Wi-Fi in a smaller apartment or as a backup unit. Setup wasn't totally plug-and-play for me, the quick-start guide skipped a few steps, so I ended up digging into the manual config page. Once I got it dialed in, signal's been stable and speeds are fine for streaming and casual gaming. I wouldn't push it hard with a ton of devices or expect blazing range, but for light-to-moderate home use, it does the job. Recommended for budget-conscious folks comfortable with a little tinkering.

I wasn't sure what to expect since I'd been using cheaper nonstick pans forever, but this Viking pan honestly blew me away. Right out of the box it felt solid without being stupidly heavy. First thing I made was a mushroom frittata, cooked it on medium-low, and the heat spread so evenly there were zero scorched spots, which never happens with my old pans. Cleanup took like ten seconds with a sponge. The high sides are a bonus I didn't think I'd care about, but they actually keep my stovetop way cleaner. It's one of those rare purchases where you immediately wonder why you waited so long to upgrade.
Fits perfectly and the touch sensitivity is great, but the application process is a bit tricky.
Has a cracking pace that makes it hard to put down.

This book was given to me in exchange for an honest review. It follows on and answers a lot of the questions posed in part one. It has hot sex scenes and lots of dramatic sequences. The character Justice, seemed a bit of a loose canon, for some one who needed protection, she seemed to wander freely, with no concern for those doing the right thing by her. This is not normally my genre, but for light hearted reading, I enjoyed it.
For a few bucks, this story stuck with me way longer than most full-price novels. It's a quick, satisfying read that honestly made me rethink what "enough" really means. Totally worth it.

I've had Jillian's 30 Day Shred DVD for over three years now and it's still going strong, no skips, no scratches, and I've moved it between three different houses. I originally grabbed it to get back in shape after my second kid, and honestly, I keep coming back to it whenever I fall off the wagon. The disc itself has held up way better than some pricier workout sets I've bought, even with my toddler getting his hands on it once or twice. After all this time, the menu loads fast and the chapters still skip cleanly, which is more than I can say for my streaming options when the wifi acts up. It's just a solid, reliable disc that's earned a permanent spot on my shelf.
My son love the Punk Rock look, so every design bracelets and rings he have.
My toddler loves these little wooden figures, keeps her busy for hours.

I really wanted to love this, especially since it's priced pretty fairly for how long one bottle lasts. I've been adding it to my dog's water for about three weeks now. The minty scent is pleasant and her breath definitely smells fresher, which is a nice bonus. As far as actual tartar control goes, though, I'm not seeing much difference, her teeth look about the same as before, so it's not a replacement for brushing. She drinks it without any fuss, so at least palatability isn't an issue. For the cost, it's an okay breath freshener, but if you're hoping for real dental results, you might end up a little disappointed like I was. I'd say it's decent value if you just want a minor breath boost.

Got this as a little gift for my niece and honestly, for the price it's a steal. The fabric feels way nicer than expected, super gentle and cozy. She toddles around in the robe like a little champ. Washes well too, no pilling. Definitely one of those finds where the quality punches above the cost.
Surprisingly strong, handles tougher jobs than my old cordless drill.

These have held up okay after a couple months of regular backyard use, but I'm noticing some fraying already on the lines near the top ring. The metal connector they include is pretty flimsy and started to rust after the first rain, so I swapped it out for a small carabiner I had lying around. The hoop itself is still intact, but the string tension loosened up faster than I expected, and I've had to re-tie a few spots just to keep things from tangling mid-throw. For casual tossing they're fine, but if you're practicing daily, the wear shows quicker than I'd like. Decent for the cost, just don't expect them to stay crisp for long.
The squeaker was dead in under two minutes, and my golden retriever barely chews. The fabric also started fraying immediately. Total waste for something marketed as durable.

This is your basic bit set and will get the job done for most projects. The quality is good but not excellent. The magnetic base is the best feature. I like the quick change feature of the insert but this adds a certain amount of "slop" to the setup. Realizing these limitations, this is a pretty good setup for the average do-it-yourselfer.

Gets the job done protect the tank well. I droped my thank on concrete and this case saved it from a sure crack so that too mee is a product that does what itwas designed to do. Be advised the thank will not fit in a vest with the cover on and anything bogger than a 77cc will not be entirely protected. Love it looks great and would be perfect if you attach tank to gun directly and would also be a must to protect an expensive tank.

This thing is awesome! I would buy again if I could carry two. I keep finding out things about it. It feels good loaded and believe me I have had it loaded. I will be adding a hydration bladder later. I like the gun holder feature and the fact that you can easily remove the pack from the frame. The zip up pockets on the waste belt are very handy, maps, small binoculars, multi tool etc. seems very well made. I use the shelf for for sleeping bag however, the pack has a sleeping bag compartment. It is basically a pack inside a pack with drawstrings at the top and bottom. If you use the shelf for your bag you can access everything from the bottom. I replaced the the round key ring type things that the pack is attached with with cotter type pins but I understand their idea behind the rings. They are a little quieter. I sprayed it down with Kiwi Camp dry for good measure. As you can tell I am very pleased with my purchase and would give six stars if I could. Next I would like to try their brand of sleeping pad.

This set includes 5 different animals that are on the endangered species list. There is a black rhinoceros, manatee, giant panda, cheetah, and albatross. The animals all have great detail. The wrinkles on the rhino and manatee are realistic. They are well made from a thick plastic. The set also comes with an activity guide. The activity guide gives lots of great information about each of the animals in the set. It gives information like why the animal is endangered, where they live, and special characteristics. It also gives discussion starters and a couple of activities. I opened it up and my son immediately asked questions. He started to name the couple he knew. I corrected or helped with the couple he didn't know. We talked about the animals and a little about each. I told him they are all endangered and of course he asked what that meant. We talked a little about them, but mostly he just likes to pretend play with them. He was pretending today that the manatee was swimming really fast and then he broke his tail so he was swimming slow and then it got better and he went fast. Such a 4 year old play game. There is so much play that can happen with these animals. Pretend play is such a valuable skill to learn and why not add in some other learning. The Jumbo Endangered Animals are for ages 2 years and up. I really think they can be used for many years. There are so many learning uses for these animals. Any child that likes animals would love these. They would make a great gift.

I grabbed these for a full brake line replacement on my old pickup, and the 10mm wrench gripped the stubborn bleeder screws like a champ. The fit is snug enough that I didn't round off a single fitting, even on the ones that looked rust-welded in place. The satin finish feels good in hand and wipes clean without rust spots showing up later. My only minor gripe is the roll-up pouch is a bit flimsy, but the wrenches themselves are solid. For the price, they punch way above their weight and saved me a ton of frustration compared to using standard open-end wrenches.
Good quality and grandson loved crawling through it. Folds up nicely so it doesn't take up a lot of space.
These roll smooth on pavement and cut road noise way more than I expected, only wish they gripped a bit better on damp paths.
3.5 Stars. Worked ok (better than Polysporin) for my German Shepherd's eye infections due to his allergies.

I've been hooked on this series since book one, and number thirteen just proves why I keep coming back. The way the story keeps tightening its grip around the characters is so well done, I finished it in a single sitting because I honestly couldn't put my Kindle down. There's this perfect blend of tension, heat, and emotional pull that just works. I love how the relationships keep evolving without losing that raw intensity. And that ending? Totally wrecked me in the best way, I'm already counting down to the next installment. If you enjoy stories that keep your heart racing and your mind spinning, this series is a must. Easily one of my favorite reading escapes this year.
These treats still smell fresh and break cleanly even after a month in the treat jar. My picky pup goes nuts for them every single time.
I chose the book because I loved others books written by Howatch, but this one unfortunately disapointed me a lot.

My cat liked it fine at first, but after a few weeks the pull tab on several cans snapped clean off. Had to pry them open with a spoon, which is super annoying. Food itself is decent for the price, but the packaging durability just isn't there.

For the price, I wasn't expecting luxury, but these are surprisingly comfy. The clasp feels secure, and they've held up fine after a few wears. Not real wood, which I figured at this cost, but they look decent enough. Solid grab if you want something easy without spending much.
Not the easiest protector to install but once it's on you'll barely know it's there
Arrived fast in perfect shape, decent flick for a lazy night.

I expected an easy wireless setup, but the connection kept dropping every few minutes. Tried moving it closer, resetting it, nothing helped. It's basically unusable for my home office. Really disappointed compared to the simple USB adapter I had before.

It was really cute, but good only for a younger or smaller toddler. My son just turned three (39", 39lb) when I ordered it; I returned it as I did not see him getting much use out of it (too small to work for long).
right side of head phones just stopped working and the ear buds keep falling out.
These sandals run a touch big, so I'd suggest sizing down. The leather is soft and they feel way more expensive than they cost.

I wore this dress twice, once for a dinner and again for a friend's party, and the seams are already pulling apart at the sides. After one gentle hand wash, the fabric pilled horribly and the hem stitching unraveled. It looks cheap and worn out now, definitely not built to last. Super bummed because I really liked the style, but the quality is awful for the price.
Fits perfectly over the crate and gives our pup a cozy den feel. Looks way tidier than our old sheet setup.

These are super cute, very comfortable and warm. I don't like to wear them barefoot as I can feel the seams, but with a pair of socks they are just right. Fun slippers, well crafted. I've gotten compliments from friends and family.

My kids are obsessed with the color-changing panels, every throw lands on a new bright shade and they can't stop giggling. I was worried it'd be too heavy for my three-year-old, but it's super lightweight and soft enough for indoor play without breaking anything. The built-in rattle noise is oddly soothing, not annoying at all. We've taken it to the park, the beach, even grandma's house, and it's held up perfectly. Simple, durable, and genuinely entertaining.
The curved design actually stays put during my morning runs.
I went in expecting the usual facts, but it completely blew me away with fresh stories.

I grabbed this little light mainly out of curiosity after seeing how thin the Cardsharp knife was, and honestly it's been a sleeper hit for me. The beam is surprisingly focused for something this slim, lights up a dark hallway way better than my phone ever could. I keep it in my wallet's card slot and forget it's there until I need it. Used it crawling under the sink last week and the twist switch didn't accidentally activate in my pocket beforehand, which is a huge plus. Battery life seems solid too, haven't changed it yet. For the size and price, it's kind of ridiculous how handy this thing is.

I purchased the H80 model when I decided to upgrade to a better case. I was unfamiliar with liquid cpu coolers, so I didn't really know what to expect. It comes with several different fittings, so you can be sure that it will fit on your motherboard. The important thing to do when researching these is to make sure you have the correct space to fit the radiator. I opted for the H80, and I'm glad I did. It seems to be very well made, I checked all the seams to make sure there were no leaks, and I checked the hoses for any cuts, and all was in perfect order. The included instructions are kind of a joke, there a are only pictures showing the different setup for the various fittings, they don't actually explain how to install it, but it is pretty self explanatory if you have some tech sense. All in all I am pretty happy with the purchase, I have had no problems with it, and it has kept my cpu cooler than my old cpu fan, even during long gaming sessions and photo and video editing. The one thing that makes me give it a four star rating instead of a five star rating are the fans and the connectors. The connectors are 3 pins instead of 4, which means the fans will run on full sail the entire time. They end up sounding like a jet engine, and was so loud I could hear it downstairs through my floor. I bought a NZXT fan controller, and was able to turn them down. The noise is much more acceptable. Corsair could and should have spent the little bit more money and made the connectors 4 pin.
For use at granddaughters college dorm bed. She says it really improves her sleep.

Holds the morrell elite super duper good & is very sturdy. The super duper bag does rest on the bottom bar of the stand just a bit though. Went together easy & going on 3 months of heavy use with no issues so far!

It is definitely not as big and strong as the industrial cages you find at the gym, but its perfect for a workout room or basement at home. The cage is relatively light weight, I can easily slide it around or pick it up and move it. If you are throwing up any amount of weight, the entire cage slides backwards when you throw the bar back to re-rack it on the barbell catches. It would start sliding on me, but I just pushed it up against the wall. But the primary reason for only 3 stars is the bench. The base of the bench is a T shape. This is great if you are laying back as the legs the jet out provide support. But when you are sitting up or using the leg extension, you are basically sitting on a rail. It is wobbily and not sturdy feeling at all. After two months I have gotten used to it, but I wish the bench was of higher quality. The seat portion of the bench has 3 settings to incline it, but they are about a quarter inch apart. The entire seat barely inclines an inch. When incline benching its hard to fight the sensation that your body wants to slide down the bench. Also, raising the back is not the easiest thing. It takes some force. And finally, the wheels on the back which allow you to roll it down are terrible. I am 6'3 and I have to lift the bench way up high just to get the wheels to hit the ground. My wife who is 5'5 can't get it to roll on the wheels. She practically has to lift the end above her head. We purchased a rubber mat to put under the unit, so it is a pain to slide the bench on and off the rubber mat. The cage is great though, its everything I needed for a basement workout area.
People need to know this man His many inventions Thomas Edison did not create light bulb

....."Does for bathtubs what Psycho did for showers"...that was the promo for this picture...to compare this picture to Psycho is borderline sacrilege........Ford and Pfeiffer turn in good workmen-like performance but the flaw in this movie is in its slow moving plodding direction. It is mysterious and spooky but spine tingling edge-of-the-seat suspenseful it is not......Until the final scenes where Ford is exposed as a killer with a murder in his past does the movie exhibit any real tension but it takes a long time getting there and if you haven't fallen asleep by then you might enjoy this over hyped movie......Fans with a foot fetish might enjoy the copious shots of Pfeiffer's bare feet.

I returned the game because it was poorly made. The holes that were supposed to be in the center of the discs were off-center, so they didn't stack properly. Some of the holes weren't cut big enough, so they didn't even fit on the pegs. The pegs don't fit well into the holes that were cut for them, so they fall out easily while the game is being played.

Really sharp watch - works well for more dressy or casual events - I needed a watch with a very light brown or camel colored band to match belts and shoes I wear and this one does the trick - Timex usually has served me well so I am sure this one will be no different!
My cat loves this - she thinks she is the queen sitting on the top. She plays with the toy and uses the scratching post. It looks nice too.

I got my backpack, tried to put my 5lb chihuahua in it and it doesn't look anything like the pictures on the page. The sizes aren't accurate. I wasn't able to tighten the hole small enough so that my dog wouldn't fall out. I did not feel like my dog was safe in this carrier. Also, the first time I used it, I zipped it up once and when I unzipped it THE ZIPPER FELL OFF. I tried to get a refund through Amazon but I had to go through this third party seller. It was a huge hassle. I had to send them pictures of the broken zipper and they had issues opening the picture file even though it was a picture I took on my smart phone and sent it to them directly in an email. They would not take a return or give me a full refund. They offered to refund $5 to my credit card to make up for the broken zipper. I told them that was not acceptable and then they stopped responding. Beware of this seller. The company does not stand behind their products. If I NOT could raye them less tham 1 star, I would. I DO NOT AT ALL RECOMMENDED!!!
Worked fine for a couple months, then the zoom ring got so stiff I could barely turn it. Cheap build, total waste.
This book will make you feel dirty from the first page on. Like gotta take a shower but you still can't get the filth off you. Loved it.

I keep this in my tool bag for cabinet installs and tight corner work, total game changer. You have to brace it a bit when driving, but the magnet holds bits and screws securely so nothing flops around. If you do any trim, furniture assembly, or work inside cabinets, grab one. I've already told my brother-in-law to get his own.
I love it. This one has an extra step, three instead of two with my other one. All parts fit securely, so it's not easy to figure out if you don't know the procedure.
Liked everything but the brush, it was cheap and fell apart

I LOVE LOVE LOVE this LED CRLight 4W Dimmable LED Filament Candle Light Bulb,2700K Warm White 400LM, E12 Candelabra Base Lamp C35 Bullet Top, Clear Glass Cover, 40W Incandescent Replacement light! This 4 Watt is exactly what I've been looking for and an amazing light bulb! The placement of where we use this light is in our bedroom fan light. I feel so much better knowing that we are only using much less power instead of full 40 wattage of an incandescent bulb. I have attached several pictures for comparison between the original style light bulb and this E12 base dimmable candelabra bulb. The first picture shows the size comparison between the 40W incandescent light bulb and this 4W LED candle light bulb (practically the same size). They have the same E12 base size and these light bulbs are for light fixtures such as chandeliers, fans, etc (not for tiny night lights). The construction and quality of the light bulb is so sturdy and excellent. The other picture shows how the LED light bulb glows and puts off a ton of light. This particular bulb is a Warm White which is my favorite and preferred choice of light bulb color. Ive come to realize the Bright White bulbs look too blue for my taste and the Warm yellow looks too much like my grandmas house. So my preference is in the middle at the warm white. I tested the dimmable feature of these light bulbs as our fans have dimmable remotes to control the light. There is no flicker in the bulb at all and looks like a normal bulb when dimming. I think having the LED light with the characteristic of being dimmable is a huge plus since our fan has a remote with a dimmer switch and it works like a charm. I like the option to dim whenever I want to. I wish I would have bought this light sooner and started saving my energy bills! I did receive this product at a discounted rate in exchange for my honest review/opinion.
Box was a little beat up, but the item works fine.

I bought this antenna in hopes of receiving more OTA TV. But ended up only getting about 2 channels that didn't seem to work half the time. I switched to the Winegard Metrostar 2000, and have had a lot better success.
Things are starting to heat up now and secrets are coming out. Will he reveal all of them and is he really falling for her or is it just a ruse. I'm intrigued to find out.
Good fit and comfortable, I guess the "all day pant" title is well earned. Already bought another pair now that I know that they fit well.
Wow, talk about fast paced, this book was really good & I liked it. One thing about money......greed, deceit, murder & scheming is sure to follow & this book had it all.

I grabbed this to hook up an older Dell monitor with only DVI input to my newer workstation. The latch mechanism on the DisplayPort end is a standout, it clicks in securely and stays put, no wiggling loose when I'm rearranging my desk. I've had zero flickering or signal dropouts at 1920x1200, and text looks razor-sharp. Cable itself is flexible enough to route cleanly behind my stand without fighting me. Didn't need any adapters or driver fiddling, just plug and go. For a straightforward single-cable solution that just works, this thing is perfect. Highly recommend if you're reviving a solid DVI monitor.
I've worn this belt daily for hikes and range days, and it hasn't sagged or slipped once. The stiffness holds my gear perfectly, yet it's comfortable enough to forget I'm wearing it all day.

I'll be damned if the secrets and treachery wasn't at an all time high. Toni had me confused for a minute but I kept reading to make sure I wasn't going crazy. Brielle, Chelle and Meachy all found themselves caught up but I wasn't expecting the secrets that came about. Blake was just trifling and doing the most. Here I thought he was messing up one situation but so much more came with it. Brielle was wrong but it was nothing she could do to change the way things panned out. I liked Brandon and his boldness at dinner and that really had me cracking up. I was pissed about the lie he told at his office but I guess he was doing what he thought was best. Eliana seemed like a very sweet person and I hate how what she thought was something special was built from lies. Chelle and Meachy had me so confused but once I started understanding what was what it really threw me for a loop. Meachy's role wasn't excused but she didn't have to let everybody else's secrets out to save her own ass. I'll admit I had a wtf look once Chelle found out Brielle's secret because I was thinking how in the world can she be mad when she's no better. Monique was bitter and wanted her cake and eat it too. Her response to KJ let me know she didn't have it all. Once the secrets started flowing it was like they'd never end. So much trust was broken but the very secrets that destroyed so much also had some good to come about. As always great job Toni and I can't wait to see what you come out with next.
I actually had one dose without any medication in it. I m sure it was a manufacturing snafu but none the less.... Also, it works but doesn t seem to last for 30 days...more like 2.5 to 3 weeks.

Arrived a little banged up in a bubble mailer, but the spine was intact. Started strong, then fizzled out way too early.
Love this vest for working outdoors, fits great and feels tough.

Blended stories of four fascinating protagonists. Written after a vast amount of research, this reads like a mystery and features lots of history, great characters and even romance. Who knew these remarkable women were brave, resourceful and charming under fire? Inspiring nail-biter! Linda Donaldson
Not for what it said, does not fit Samsung Galaxy s6 edge plus as stated.

I was intrigued and so bought this book, only then I realised how interesting the book and the subject was. It's a tough read stepping in a mentally ill mind but interesting to see the way they think. Good read, very informative.
My dog's been gnawing on the same puck for a week and it's still in one solid piece.
I've re-read this paperback three times and the spine still looks nearly new.
Box arrived crushed and the parts inside were already scuffed up. Super flimsy, not worth even trying to assemble.

I've been using this pack for my daily bike commute for about two months now, and honestly, it's even better than I expected. The build quality feels super solid, no loose threads or cheap zippers anywhere. I was worried it might feel bulky, but it sits comfortably even when fully loaded. The organization is spot-on; I can stash my laptop, change of clothes, lunch, and bike tools without things getting jumbled. That hidden rain cover already saved me twice during surprise downpours, and everything inside stayed bone dry. It's one of those rare buys where the actual experience matches the product photos and description perfectly. Definitely worth every penny.
The motor started making a loud grinding noise after just three days. The fountain looks nice, but the constant racket is unbearable. Totally useless now.

My 1 yr old son received this as a gift. Wish I wouldn't have tossed the box so we could return it! It collapses to easily (just while playing), the gears do not stay on- as soon as you turn either on the end they ALL pop off, and the nails pretty much fall right into the holes. If you are looking for a toy for your little boy (or girl ) to tinker around with I highly suggest the LeapFrog Fix and Learn Speedy. The drivers are a wrench and screw driver that are very toddler friendly to grasp and 'work' with, cutesy (not annoying) songs, it cues when something on Speedy breaks and needs some fixing and of course doubles as a race car. Speedy gets played with daily by my son (which says a lot for a toy). If you have a toddler that is more interested in tinkering rather than necessarily playing (like my little guy), check out Speedy, NOT this workbench!
The author transports you right to the streets of Savannah. The characters are delightfully complex and the story grips you from start to finish.
Great story with characters who stay consistent, yet grow throughout the series. Fun magical adventures in a well-written story capture the imagination.

Got this set in two days, which was a nice surprise. Packaging was simple but effective, each cord was individually banded and nothing was tangled or scuffed on arrival. The plastic storage jar is sturdier than I expected and fits neatly in my truck toolbox without rolling around. First impression: the hooks feel solid, and the cords stretch smoothly with no gritty resistance. I've only used a couple so far for securing a tarp and strapping down a toolbox in the bed, and they held tight. I'm docking one star because the smaller cords are a little stiff out of the jar, but I think they'll loosen up. Overall, a handy grab-and-go kit that seems built to last.

A Stairway to Danger is an intriguing novel filled with action and adventure from beginning to end. Tom and Will, two cousins living in a small town in the 1920s, become involved in a mystery when they find the body of the local deputy. Soon, they are investigating a mysterious barge on the river and becoming entangled in a dangerous adventure. I found this novel to be reminiscent of the Hardy Boy series, except it is geared toward an older audience. The characters are well-thought out and realistic and the author gives the reader a good sense of the time period. Ben Woodard is an excellent writer who knows how to plot and plan a great adventure story.

Cable works great and appears to be of high quality. My one issue with it is the video wire is thicker than the audio wires, making it stiffer which created a tendency to pull on one of the audio jacks so it would come loose from the plug. I positioned the cable in a way that provided enough slack in the audio wires, but it was a nuisance to deal with.

Thought I'd save some cash skipping the brand-name glass, but this thing is a total letdown. Edges are dark and mushy no matter the aperture, colors look like they've been through a muddy filter, and anything off-center is just blur soup. Tried it on two bodies, same sad results. Honestly, I'd only recommend this lens to someone who needs a prop for a film set or a paperweight, definitely not for actual shooting. Even beginners deserve better; you'll just end up frustrated and re-buying the real deal anyway. Lesson learned the expensive way.

Cathy Johnson was busy helping out the family restaurant and trying out new things to add to the menu to think about the TV production that is coming to their small town in Virginia. The whole town seemed to be pretty much excited except her. Her heart has been broken by her high school sweetheart, a relationship that ended in a disaster. The last thing she needs was to become star struck. Until she crossed path with the sexy and charming Greg Moran who arrives in Dogwood together with the rest of the cast and crew of Bittersweet. They had this crazy connection together, but if Cathy was careful to get her emotions in tangle, Greg can be pretty much fickle minded. He can be all in, and then all of a sudden, he was nowhere in sight. He can be interested now, and then appear disinterested the next time. He had some secrets of his own, that I wanted to know. I would have loved it more if he gets his own POV. But I was stuck with Cathy, and her thoughts weren't that bad. There were a lot of bumps before their happy ever after and there were times when youd just wanted to ask Greg if he really wants Cathy, or if both of them wanted this to work There were times when I got frustrated with the hot-then-cold Greg just about everything seems to be perfect, suddenly theyd be back to square one, but still I found this book romantic and endearing. I love Cathy, and her friend Max and Hal they were a great bunch, and both of her friends are there to catch her every time she falls. Greg was hard to love, but you couldn't resist his charms, so please don't blame Cathy. This is my first Sunday James read, and it was okay. I look forward for the next book in the series and cant wait to read whats next for this two!

I bought these as a replacement for an older pair of Sony earbuds. I don't know if this pair will eventually present the same problem, but my experience with Sony earbuds has been very mediocre. First the good. The sound is decent, though nothing special, and the fit is pretty good. They are very comfortable and don't fall out, even when I'm jogging and sweaty. My issue with Sony earbuds is that (with every pair I've owned over the last 10 years) the sound in one ear eventually goes out, never to return. Then they're garbage. My friends have all reported having the same problem and none of us has been able to figure out what causes this problem. It does not appear to be something you can control by treating the earburs with special care of any sort. I personally believe that Sony designs them to die on you, so that you'll constantly have to keep spending another $20 to replace them. For my next pair (when these inevitably meet their one eared fate), I'm thinking of going higher end for a pair of earbuds that will last. Do I recommend these as a pair of disposable earbuds that may give you 6 good months? Yes. Do I recommend them for longer? No.

ups dollied it up to my garage door, and rolled it inside after ringing door bell. Box was manufactured box and Styrofoam was molded to protect and surround the 100 pound Boss log splitter. It was simple to open box and remove machine from box. A+ I read directions in manual just because wise thing to do. I opened the fly nut 6 or 7 times and ran it until air was out of hydraulic line. After retightened I decided to check oil on the stick after reading reviews about oil being at lowest line from factory. I tilted it up on end and put 7mm hex in nut and will not move. I mean will not turn loose. So I called 800 number. Got a very pleasant gentlemen on phone that told me that's because they use thread lock so it will not leak in transit. He said you have to give the hex wrench a hard tap to break the glue or use a lock creasant wrench (vise-grips) to lock on the round end of hex dip stick and give that a tap to loosen. This is not in the manual. Word of advise: Do not strip your 7mm hex nut, use the lock wrench and tap it and behold it is loose. Then screw it out with hex wrench that is included. I found the hydrologic oil to still be between the lines. Then I spent about 3 hours splitting about half a cord of wood. it went through my hard sweet gum ball wood like butter( if you get it with the grain). Stacked it and covered it and done. Also use some kind of silicone spray on the black and red back and forth stuff. Oil will cause wood chips to stick to it. And it helps to prevent screeching sounds. Great price, great design, great one handed control. I'll never wear this thing out. **Copy received in return for an honest review** This is a great standalone story and book three in the series. Book One introduced the concept of two worlds post war, divided by hidden portals. Cascadia is a world without technology or motorized vehicles and it citizens have paranormal abilities the Pacificans covet. For years the Pacifican Army has created crime waves they attribute to the Cascadians, giving them the reason they need for sustaining funding and support to continue their troop numbers. They also seek out descendents of Cascadians living in Pacifica who have paranormal abilities and force them into the army. Those who refuse are incarcerated. The more promising of these Talents are sent to a research lab, The Institute. That is where Vince Crawford has been held for the past ten years, refusing to give up information about a portal he discovered that led him to Cascadia and Zara Kane, the girl he fell in love with. Zara had just told Vince she was pregnant when he disappeared. Thinking he abandoned her, she is shocked to learn he has been imprisoned the whole time. Fiercely determined to free him, she convinces her brother to help and uses her cloaking Talent to get Vince away from his work detail and to a safe house. But neither of the lovers are the same people they were before Vince was taken. Their resulting story is one of bravery, love and patience in the face of Vince's PTSD and the force of the Pacifican Army intent on recapturing their most valuable prisoner.

I've always had a soft spot for classic family films, but this one genuinely surprised me with how well it holds up. The villain is wonderfully over-the-top, and the musical numbers add a cozy, storybook feel that's perfect for holiday viewing. I put this on for nostalgia's sake and ended up completely hooked. Like many other reviewers, I can confidently rate this tablet with at least 4 stars...provided you're a geek who is comfortable rooting your devices and installing new ROMs. As nearly anyone who has used this tablet will tell you, out of the box it's complete crap. How Viewsonic managed to so badly mangle the operating system is beyond me. But...spend just a couple minutes on the xda forums to find the instructions for how to root, spend another couple of minutes performing the root and ROM installation (and, for extra credit, install the Pershoot kernel), and you'll have a tablet that is lightening quick and capable. I am currently running the latest VEGAn ROM (Gingerbread flavored) with the Pershoot kernel overclocked to 1.4 GHz. With a couple of battery-draining apks (applications) deleted, I'm still seeing amazing battery life and applications launch immediately. A couple of notes if you're one of those people who has been slobbering after the latest "fruity" tablet... This ain't no iPad. But that's only because the Android OS is still transforming from it's caterpillar form into the beautiful butterfly it will become. You'll trade "pretty" for "powerful" since a rooted tablet like this one will give you full access to all of the amazing (and often experimental) apps in the Android Market. Another ding: accessories. I picked up the USB keyboard/case for this device on amazon (for about $25) and love it. It adds amazing functionality with complete plug-and-play ability. But...that's about the *only* case you'll find for it besides the generic sleeves for 10" tablets (I also got one of those). I would be remiss if I didn't mention that the dreaded poor viewing angle for the screen often annoys me. It's true that you could potentially claim this is a "privacy" feature, but don't be fooled. I've often had to tweak the angle to get a comfortable non-eye-strain producing view of the screen for long periods. Lastly, the front-facing camera (the only one) stinks. But, at a price half to a third of similar tablets on the market, I'm OK with that and the other limitations I mentioned.

Due to the quirks of the marketplace, this was available at a much lower price than the 6" and 8" wrenches in the same series. The one I received was remarkable in that the jaws were very, very close to parallel. Most wrenches of this type, whether foreign or domestic, tend to have jaws that are closer together at the opening tip than deep towards the throat, thus requiring the user to lift up the wrench axially after each turn for repositioning, rather than pulling the wrench radially away from the nut. If there is no room for this, then the jaws need to be loosened to remove the wrench, and retightened in the new position. Parallel jaws, or jaws that are wider at the mouth than throat, saves the extra steps. The post that holds the worm gear adjuster was only lightly staked, and unscrews to allow maintenance. Due to manufacturing tolerances, however, there is no assurance that yours won't be impossible to unscrew, or that the jaws will be parallel.

Originally released in 1934 as "Babes in Toyland," this film is far and away the best of the Laurel & Hardy operettas. The evil Barnaby (Henry Brandon), wants to force the lovely Bo-Beep (Charlotte Henry) to marry him. Towards this end Barnaby banishes Bo-Beep's beloved Tom-Tom (Felix Knight) and attempts to take over Toyland with his demon army. Fortunately, Stannie Dum and Ollie Dee, the assistants of the Toymaker (William Burgess), save the day with the help of the marvelous giant-sized Wooden Soldiers. Based on the operetta by Victor Herbert (several songs are omitted), this film was directed by Gus Meins and Charles Rogers, and is much superior to the Disney version that ended up replacing it on television. You will find less slapstick than usual in a Laurel & Hardy film, but the boys are better integrated into this musical than any of there others and Brandon's Barnaby is deliciously wicked. Fortunately "March of the Wooden Soldiers" has been restored and is now available so it can become the classic it deserves to be.
