I went into this expecting a dry business book, but it completely flipped how I think about building anything new. The core idea, treating your startup like a science experiment with validated learning, sounded simple, but the way Ries breaks it down with real stories made it stick. I've already started using the Build-Measure-Learn loop on a side project, and it's saving me from wasting weeks on features nobody wants. It's not just theory; it's super actionable, even if you're not in tech. Honestly, I highlighted so many passages my copy looks like a coloring book. If you're tired of guessing what customers need and want a clear framework, this is the one. Way more practical than I ever expected.

Private Investigator V.I. Warshawski receives a call from her old high school basketball coach, Mary Ann McFarlane who has been sidelined with cancer. She wants V.I. to help her coach 4 hours a week. V.I. takes on the job and is drawn into the lives of her players. Josie Dorrado's mother Rose begs her for help as the factory where she works has been sabotaged. Frank Zamar, the factory owner, is angry with V.I., and refuses her help as he is obviously terrified of something. V.I.'s private life is also in turmoil. The man in her life, Morrell, an investigative reporter, is at home recovering from a serious gunshot wound; and Marcena Love, an attractive and gutsy colleague, is staying with him. Marcena is trying to come up with a story on South Chicago and uses V.I.'s work with the basketball team as a way to get to know people. She ends up getting very close to the player's fathers. The team needs a real coach, a new gym floor and equipment, so V.I. begins to search for corporate sponsors. She meets the grandson of the famous Bill Bysen, the head of a large conservative huge department store chain called By-Smart. Unlike his family, Billy is an idealist that can see how his family's business practices are harming the neighborhood. Billy brings the pastor of the South Chicago church where he is working to preach at the company headquarter's morning prayer meeting. This really antagonizes the family when the preacher lectures them on their responsibility to the poor and the community. Armchair Interviews says: Paretsky has another hit here. Her usual cast of incredibly wonderful characters and realistic depictions of big city life make this book a true joy to read.

I've read most of the Bosch series, and this one hits differently because of how personal the cold case feels. The way the investigation peels back layers of a kid's tragic past really got to me, Connelly balances the procedural grind with genuine emotional weight. I only knock it down a star because the middle section drags a bit with some overly detailed forensic back-and-forth that felt like filler. Still, the final act pays off hard, and the atmosphere of old Hollywood secrets kept me hooked. Solid entry for longtime fans.

It hasn't hit overload yet, but I fear it might if it continues throughout the series. Overall, this was a great 5-star read for me.
Came a little bent but readable, started slow, then just when it got good it ended.

I have ordered a ton of these as baby gifts and children's gifts for my almost 2 y.o. son and all the birthdays we will be invited to! The Jellycat Bashful series is completely adorable and extremely soft, but I believe the bunnies are hands-down the cutest/softest!

I bought two. They have looked great on both my vehicles for several months. Now, I just wish people knew what it was around here. Update: After almost a year more, they still are holding on and looking great! Go Tigers!
Very handy for tightenning accessories and components. Looks to be well made. I like the fact that it splits in two parts which makes it easier to work with.
A few new plot twists and a great love story. I would recommend this book to any one who enjoy a good romance and shifter story.
For under twenty bucks, these are a steal. Solid grip and they feel way more expensive than they are.
I bought two of these and I really like them; better yet my dogs like them. I take them hunting and they stack well and hold up in cold weather.

I love westerns and the premise drew me in. This is the first book I've read by Elizabeth Rose and I am looking forward to reading more. She captured the Old West with a descriptive and believable setting. Rena dresses as a boy and helps her brother, who is falsely convicted of murder, escape the gallows. Now she has the bounty hunter on her tail! I felt connected to the spunky heroine and the hero who is surprisingly a bounty hunter. While this was a clean romance I did feel the sparks building at the end of the story and had wished it was a bit longer to show more of the budding romance.

Got 32GB of this DDR3 for an older budget build and honestly didn't expect much at this price point. It's easily half what other kits cost. Plugged into an ASRock board, auto-detected the 1600MHz profile straight away, no fiddling in BIOS needed. Timings settled at CL11 right out of the box, rock stable through a full weekend of gaming and memtest runs. Zero crashes, zero weirdness. For the money, this is kind of a steal, makes refreshing an aging system actually affordable. Load times in my heavily modded Skyrim setup dropped noticeably compared to the mismatched 4GB sticks I had before. If you're on the fence, just grab it.

Ordered 3 (in yellow). Rec'd 2 but also 1 different ViewHD product that had a label slapped on it indicating it was what I had ordered... NOT! After explaining this very confusing situation to Amazon's now-barely-English-speaking cust. service, and because this is Amazon Marketplace and not Amazon, I have to keep the wrong thing (for which I have no use whatsoever), wait for a refund, and place a new order for the 3rd. I'm avoiding "the Marketplace" at all costs in the future.
Looks decent in photos but the whole thing feels flimsy in person. Lid sticks constantly and the plastic body already has a crack.
Decent enough for the price, but it drags compared to earlier books. For what I paid, it's okay to borrow from a friend rather than own.
Fits slightly small. Other than that, it is very very cute with vibrant colors.

Georgette St. Clair absolutely nails the banter between her leads in this one, I was laughing out loud one minute and fanning myself the next. The small-town shifter vibe is so cozy, yet the chemistry is off the charts.

I ordered a half size bigger after reading the reviews here. They fit perfectly. Very nicely cushy for my daily 5-7 mile walks. Had been having minor knee and back pain which seem to be much better since I got these shoes. Very satisfied.
I gave this as a gift and Emme LOVED it, she was strutting and had a beaming smile while carrying her prized gift.

It's a slow burn that didn't hook me right away, but by episode three I was fully invested. The historical detail is richer than I expected, and the character arcs feel more grounded than the usual time-travel fare. It's not perfect, some plot threads wander, but the emotional payoff hits harder than I anticipated. A solid, thoughtful watch.
The stitching started unraveling after just one wear, total waste of money.
Ashley delivers again, solid pacing and a twist I didn't see coming. For the price, it's a total steal and kept me hooked over a lazy weekend.

What a terrible end to this series. I loved this character and her gargoyles from the first novella that I read but this book was technical with almost no heart or development for the few characters there were. I was completely bored with the formation of crystals and what it took to form the crystals. I felt like I was in a cave forming crystals through out most of the book. That is what I mean by the book being too technical. If there was such a thing as gargoyles, this would be the manual on how to make your own gargoyle. The writer spent way too much time on that and not enough time on developing the romance and bringing life to the final story in this series. I miss the people and the gargoyles from the previous books. This book felt like a novella that was dragged out.

Stories that impart to the reader the feel of the Code of Conduct that once prevailed in the nature of western American culture. I enjoyed reading all 9 novels and recommend this set to those who like a good western.

My oh my looks like Dr. Hugo is about to get the rest and relaxation he needs and what better place than at Wiccan Haus. On the way he meets Aislinn who is going going for the what if because until now nothing else has worked. Oh and she does not want to have anything else to do with doctors so Hugo best be wary. Added to the lovely mix is also Punda who is drawn to them so this author takes you with them to weave her special blend of magic to give you their story here. What will soon be discovered though for Aislinn will be hard to break but with the love she can receive from these two anything is possible. Hugo will also learn that there is more to life then mere science and that the best is yet to come. Righteous title that is fitting to say the least with the reader can find out why. Punda will also get more than she asked for or thought she would need but after you get to know her what she will get is perfect. Great battle with the winners taking the prize.
Bought for my boys. I guess they are fine. I find them in laundry so they are wearing them.

This book was as good as the first. With deputy Will, now the sheriff of Broken Spur. A green eyed beauty comes storming into Will's office, demanding the release of her father. Will is smitten at first sight, but won't release her father until he sobers up some (town rules). This just makes her madder, but she will have to return later. Read this exciting new story to find out what happens. I hope you enjoy burnt Ppl as much as I did.

Bought this to replace my Bosch 1615EVS as a dedicated table router. Has the about the same power as the Bosch and about the same amount of loud. Installed it in a JessEm Master lift. Runs with very little vibration. I can stand an unsharpened pencil on end with the motor running (no bit) on the table. I like the slide switch for the setting the motor speed.

Impressed!! Ordered from Amazon Prime, showed up right on time and packaged well.... I'm a plumber by trade, I don't deal much with this sort of thing however I know quality when I see it. This unit is built well, very easy to assemble and set up. My existing confidante drain was routed through my crawl space about 30' gravity drain through a foundation vent draining right up against the foundation. Summer heat made for LOTS of water pooling. The hornets and bugs loved the fresh water! I have a utility sink right next to my a/c coil in the garage. I set this unit up right under the a/c coil drain pan, 3/4" PVC pipe into pump container and routed the 3/8" tube into mop sink. Plugged it in, poured water in from a cup to test. It fired right up and continued to work perfect. It's very quite and it comes with the check valve so water in the tube vertical doesn't drain back into the resivoir. Very happy with it!
I read the trilogy for second time and loved it even more. Highly recommend to anyone. The writing style is gentle, detailed and captivating. Classic.

I grabbed this when it dipped to around twenty-two bucks, and at that price I can't complain too much. It's super light, which makes hauling it to the range a breeze, but the trade-off is it feels a little wobbly if you're not on flat ground. The front rest works okay for casual plinking, though I wish the padding gripped my rifle better, it tends to slide after a few shots. For the current price hovering near thirty, I'd expect a bit more sturdiness. If you catch a sale, it's a decent value, just know its limits.
Does exactly what it says, no surprises at all.
Exciting. With a great sense of humor. And a good twist to the ending. A great short very highly recommended.
Thought it'd be tighter, but the ending just felt a bit rushed.
I can say it really is a good flick of it's day and Charlton Heston really played it.

I am amazed to see a book like this all about messages in bottles, and so many interesting stories of people both putting out these bottles and the people finding them. A really original idea. The author includes some interesting history of bottle messages being used to study ocean currents centuries ago. He talks about the British navy using bottle messages for wartime communications back in the 16th century! He even has a section on 'chain letter bottles'. A very wonderful and entertaining book.
Thought I'd love it given the hype, but it just felt flat and forgettable compared to other baseball reads.

The company that supplied this "used" MBPr was extremely helpful. It wasn't really a used machine as far as I can tell. This came in the original box (more on that later) and shrunk wrapped. When I placed the order, it wasn't supposed to arrive for 4-5 days. I contacted the seller and asked if they could please ship it quicker. I also mentioned I would pay whatever the additional charge would be. They replied quickly, had it delivered in two days (NY to NJ) and there was no additional charge. I was thrilled. That is until I saw the laptop box. It was in bad shape. Not horrible, but one of the corners (and under the corner) was severely damaged. Crinkled and partially crushed. Enough so that it caused some concern about the laptops possible condition when I opened it. Turns out the laptop was in flawless condition, so no biggie. The box is still useable (although damaged) for eventual resale. I'm assuming the box was damaged BEFORE they sent it since the shipping box that contained the laptop and it's box was in great condition. Hmmm.... Anyway, it was listed as used, but there wasn't a scratch, ding or dent of any kind. Bottom line, I got a great machine with no damage for a great price with excellent customer service. No complaints. Just wish the laptop box wasn't damaged. That's the only reason I gave them 4 stars instead of 5. Highly recommend!! Great experience overall.
consider this for adult fun too! I got it as a Christmas present, but find myself tinkering with it at night when the kids have gone to bed more than i see them with it!

This is a powerful little vacuum and really is a pet hair eraser. I had to rush my german shepherd to the vet the other day and didn't have enough time to cover the car seats. Consequently, every seat in the car was literally covered in fur. I used the Bissell Pet Hair Eraser Handheld Vacuum this morning and within one hour my car looked new inside again. It has a very strong suction, and the pink rubber tip loosens the hair that sticks to the upholstery so it can be easily vacuumed. I highly recommend this little gem and the price makes it even better!

I've had this book for a few months now, and it's held up beautifully through multiple reads and a couple of accidental coffee splashes. The cover hasn't curled and the spine is still tight, no loose pages at all. I've tossed it in my bag for commutes and even lent it to a friend who's not exactly gentle with books, and it came back looking almost new. The paper quality really stands up to highlighting and margin notes without bleeding through. It's the kind of sturdy paperback you can keep on your shelf for years and it won't look beat up. Solid construction that handles real life, which I honestly didn't expect at this price.
A gift for my granddaughter after she saw mine and we both love them.

32 GB- holds a LOT of pictures and movies! Took this to England with me for an 8-day trip, took more than 700 photos and quite a few videos, and still have room for more. This is definitely the way to go! But don't forget to back up your files after you download the photos.
The blender leaks from the bottom seal every single time.
Thought it'd be a good buy but the errors make it feel overpriced for what you get.

I wasn't sure what to expect from a short story set mostly in a dentist's office, but it ended up being a fun, quirky little read. The premise is simple but clever, and the writing pulls you right into that weird, vulnerable headspace you get during a cleaning. The twist comes on gradually and left me grinning. My only small gripe is I wanted a bit more buildup in the middle, it wraps up quicker than I anticipated. Still, it's a solid pick when you need something offbeat and fast. Definitely worth the few minutes it takes to read, and I'd recommend it for a quick break.

My little guy loves the drill, it actually spins and makes noise, which keeps him busy for ages. I just wish the plastic screws stayed in place a bit better on the workbench. Still, it's sturdy enough and he plays with it daily.

For the price, I expected more. It's sturdy enough and looks fine from a distance, but the finish is already peeling after a month. Not terrible, but I probably wouldn't buy it again knowing what I know now.
I've had this peeler for about six months now and it's still going strong. The handle hasn't loosened at all and the blade stays surprisingly sharp wash after wash.

I was quite spell-bound about this book. God uses ordinary people to do extraordinary things. The lessons learned indicate that God is so alive today and speaks through his Word. Why did God use this child, one would want to know, but God uses us everyday to share his word. We are sometimes afraid to speak to others concerning how God works. I went to the website and listened to Ms. Brown share the story. As the story progressed, the reader is praying for a miracle to take place and Tara is healed completely but God had different plans.
For the cost, it's decently made. My 12-pound terrier just needs a size smaller, sadly.
The legs are longer than I thought. I expect higher quality from Danskin

Even though I measured my dog before purchasing this harness and bought the recommended size, I cannot get the front straps (where the least clips in) to be small enough to fit his body. He can fully get out of it and this is incredibly dangerous. I want a full refund!

I grabbed these Tirra slides on sale, half expecting them to be just okay for the price. Turns out they're a steal. The arch support is way better than other sandals I've spent twice as much on, and the cushioned sole handles long walks without my feet aching. They dry fast after beach trips and still look new after months of daily use. For under fifty bucks, I honestly can't believe how sturdy and well-made they feel. Total win.
I really wanted to like the sleek, compact design, but the hinge is so flimsy it popped open mid-steep and dumped wet leaves everywhere. Total mess in my mug. Looks nice, just doesn't stay shut.
Way too pricey for how buggy it is. Froze on day two and never recovered.
Picked this up for a road trip and it never let me down once. Screen is bright, directions are clear, and the battery lasts ages.

Wow, this book is amazing! Well written and technically pretty good. Bob "the Nailer" is not one of those annoyingly perfect good guys that don't really exist. He has his share of problems, which are explained in the book but not dwelled on for long. There are some pretty good reviews here that sum up the story pretty well, so I'm going to skip that. I will say that I normally read only sci-fi/fantasy and had long ago grown weary of these type of books. But the way Hunter combines the convoluted plot twists that Ludlum loves so much with the attention to detail that Clancy is so known for and then adds his own ability to tell a story in an interesting way really hooked me. You just may find yourself shopping for a Winchester mdl 70 or a Remington 700 (in.308, of course) before the end of this one! "One shot, one kill"
An easy to read book with short chapters. Carol tells interesting stories about her professional and personal life. Recommended for your reading pleasure.
These foam brushes don't shed and leave a super smooth finish every time.
Great for keeping the pond healthy, just wish the probe lasted longer.

It's among the top three favorites for the dog. She's a 20lb Shiba that destroyed the small size rather quickly so this time I got her the wolf size and it's lasting a lot longer and I think she likes having more to hold onto.

I've been using this guide off and on for a few months now, and the content itself is solid when it comes to Amazon's promotional tools. The sections on reviews and Listmania gave me actionable ideas I've already put into play. My issue is more with how the book holds up. The spine started cracking after a couple of weeks of light flipping, and now a handful of pages near the center are loose, just from normal use at my desk, nothing rough. For a reference-style book you're meant to revisit, the physical binding should last longer. The info is there, but if you're like me and like to highlight and bookmark, expect some wear and tear pretty quickly. Three stars because the advice is helpful, but the durability disappoints.

I've been hunting for a comfortable pajama set that doesn't feel flimsy after a few washes, and this Hanes set really delivered. The microfleece is surprisingly soft without being overly heavy, so it's perfect for cooler nights without making me overheat. I'm 6'0" and around 185 lbs, and the large gives me just the right relaxed fit, not baggy, not snug. The pants actually hit at my ankles properly, which is a rare win for me. The plaid pattern looks nice too, not like some faded afterthought. I've already run it through the laundry a couple times and it held up great with no pilling or weird shrinkage. For the price point, I honestly wasn't expecting much, but this set totally exceeded my expectations. Definitely grabbing another one.
Box was pretty crushed when it arrived, but the outfit inside looked okay. Not blown away though.
This was a gift to my daughter who has a dog and three cats. I can only report that she says it works great!
I was honestly expecting something flimsy but this thing feels solid and premium, totally worth it.
Super easy upgrade. I was going to help my kid do as I have an IT background but he read the directions and did it himself.
As with all your devotion books, this one did not disappoint! Great read!
For the price, it works fine, nothing fancy but does the job without breaking the bank.

I wish I could say this thing works, but after two separate attempts with a plumber friend helping, it just won't seal right. We followed the instructions exactly, made sure the flange was clean and solid, and still got a slow leak that ruined the bathroom baseboard within a week. I'd only recommend this to someone who's dealing with a temporary setup in an unfinished basement where a little water on concrete doesn't matter. For any finished space or anyone wanting a permanent fix, stay far away. Now I'm pulling the toilet again to try a wax ring like I should've from the start. Total waste.
Easy to use and works great. I read reviews on this product all over the web before buying it and agree, very good product.
It has a couple hiccups but works way better than anything else I've tried.
The lens flares were way over-the-top, but they somehow made the whole thing feel more alive and immersive. I'm not even a Trekkie and I'm hooked now.

For the price, this thing's a steal. I grabbed it mainly for Zoom calls and didn't expect much, but the image clarity honestly surprised me. It's not 4K or anything fancy, but it's sharp enough that my coworkers asked if I upgraded my whole setup. The focus ring is a nice touch too, lets me get crisp detail even when I'm holding stuff up close to show the camera. Colors look natural, and it handles my dim office lighting without turning into a grainy mess. Plug-and-play was dead simple on Windows 10, no driver headaches. At this price point, you'd be hard pressed to find anything better. Solid little workhorse.
Great little Trimmers! They're perfect for smaller sized birds! They're sharp & don't split their nails. They're just the right size for my tiels. We got them really fast & are very happy with them!

dont let the awful title fool you.it was originally called stepkids but was changed for no particular reason.dont stop watching this movie when you see the first half hour.i was about to but i decided to give it a chance to get much better.and it really did! it started to have very good cinematography or something.definetely overated.well maybe because i saw it on disney channel and they might have cut out some of the language.i really enjoyed this movie
Really cozy and looks nice, just wish it held up a bit better after a few washes. Still a solid everyday sweater.

I rated this a 5 star for several reasons: the characters are great in their parts; it offers believable situations; and while serious, there are moments of humor. Also, nice to watch a show that isn't overkill with violence.
My kid has been rough with this snake for weeks now, bending, dropping, you name it. No breaks, no paint chipping. Still looks fresh out of the box. Solid build quality, honestly.
Half of the pieces were missing magnets. Product did not meet description or expectations.

The elastic band actually stayed put all night, which I was worried about. The synthetic fibers shed a little but for a quick pirate look it totally worked. Definitely worth it for a single-use costume.
Used it for two weeks and the seams are already splitting apart.
Got this for weekend trips and it's held up great, handy, solid build, and makes fishing way more enjoyable.
Book arrived in flawless shape, packed tight with zero bends. Cracked it open the second I got it and was hooked instantly.

Spray tube was missing from the can so I have to go to a bricks & mortar store now just to get it. The cap on the can is notched to store the spray tube so someone screwed up and didn't include it in my shipment. Ugh...
Packs so small but boils water in a flash. Perfect little travel buddy for quick morning coffee anywhere.

This bracelet is really lovely in person, simple but elegant, which is exactly what I wanted for everyday wear. The packaging was a pleasant surprise; it feels more high-end than I expected for the price, with a soft pouch inside that keeps it protected when I'm not wearing it. The clasp is secure and easy to manage on my own, which is a big plus. I've worn it almost daily for a couple weeks now and haven't noticed any tarnishing or wear, so the quality seems solid. I'm taking off one star only because the chain is a tiny bit shorter than I'd prefer, it fits, but I wish it had just a little more length for a looser drape on the wrist. Still, overall a great buy.

Because the spatula is molded in one piece, it is sturdy and easy to clean. It's not as flexible as my other spatulas, but that hasn't turned out to be a problem. This thing is impervious to heat--you can use it in a pot or pan right on the stove.

I grabbed this on sale thinking it'd be a creepy period horror, but it's more of a slow, moody love story with some spooky vibes. For the discount price it's fine, looks pretty and atmospheric, just don't expect real scares. Worth a lazy evening watch, barely.
The disc played perfectly with no skips, and the case was in solid shape. A reliable purchase that made movie night easy.
I am going to buy it again when the size Med is available.

I picked this up after hearing good things, but I'm a little torn. It's accurate enough for backyard plinking and feels solid in hand, but I didn't expect the barrel-droop issue to be so noticeable out of the box. I had to shim the scope mount just to get on paper, and it still feels like I'm compensating more than I should be. I know there are fixes you can buy, but for the price I figured it'd be less of a project. My buddy's cheaper break-barrel doesn't have this quirk. When it groups, it's great, just wish the alignment was better from the start. Not a dealbreaker, but definitely a letdown.
My cat hates this thing, cheaply constructed Velcro edges are sharp on corners, and cat played with for 30 seconds the hid it from me never to find again. Garbage.

My 3-year-old is totally hooked on this set and plays with it daily. Assembly was simple enough and the variety of pieces keeps him busy for a good stretch. The trains and accessories are cute and well-sized for little hands. My only real complaint is that the track connections aren't super secure, if he leans on it or bumps it during play, sections pop apart pretty easily. We've gotten into the habit of snapping them back together, but it does interrupt the flow. Overall, it's a solid toy that sparks a lot of creative fun despite the minor frustration.

Great bike, cost a little more than you'll find in department stores, but it's a lot more bike. This bike has no foot brakes, so the rider will have to learn to use the hand brake. I purchased tektro calipers and front brake handles to install so the bike will have a front brake also, but it's not necessary because once adjusted the single rear brake works fine and is plenty strong. A note about the brake handle and little hands.. Squeeze the handle and use a n allen wrench to screw the little bolt sticking out the side of the handle, in. This makes the reach to grab the handle less. You may have to give relieve some tension on the brake cable after doing this by using the screw adjusters at the handle or at the back of the bike. The bike goes together quickly and easily. Now if you want to adjust it properly as far as the bearings, bottom bracket, headset, brake, etc, then I suggest googling Park Tools and using their tutorials, or if you have a friend into cycling have them look at it, or it having a bike shop adjust it for you. You can simply assemble it and ride it, but getting everything adjusted properly will make it last longer and ride easier. Perfect bike for kids who've outgrown the 16" bikes but are too small to enjoy riding a 20" bike.

Arrived quick and the packaging was solid, no dings or scuffs out of the box. First impression: feels sturdy with a nice finish. Mounts securely without hassle. Only wish the leg deployment wasn't limited to one direction, but overall it's a solid setup for the money.

While the ingredients are great, I have very picky bitches and neither will eat it. I even went as far as mixing their daily dosage with the entire can of their very favorite canned food, and nope, they won't touch it. Going to attempt the Bitch Pills and see if we have any luck, or will go back to OxyMate, which I firmly believe in, just wanted to give this a shot to bring in a successful heat for my older bitches last heat before retirement.
Box was a bit dented but disc played fine. First few minutes felt slow, so I'm still deciding if it gets better.
it's great, most part is as my exceptation. but, there's a small empyrosis on the lether.

I went in expecting a standard romance, but this completely wrecked me in the best way. The slow burn between Jacob and Akira felt painfully real, years of quiet glances and what-ifs finally crashing together. Rais doesn't rush a thing, and that restraint makes the payoff hit so much harder. It's tender, aching, and somehow still hopeful. Honestly, I didn't expect to cry twice before noon, but here we are. Easily my favorite read this month.

I wanted to love this top, but it's just okay. The material is breathable and has a nice softness, though it arrived pretty wrinkled. I ordered based on the size chart, and the fit is relaxed rather than the tailored look shown in the pictures. It doesn't nip in at the waist at all. For the price, it's wearable as a casual basic, but I wouldn't buy another color. Fine, not fantastic.
One of my favorite series. I will be very sad when this series ends.
the mysteries that involved a young woman who was homeless after the death of her mother.the trouble she found her self i

This little harness is way easier to get on my wiggly corgi than our old step-in one, and the padded chest piece is a nice touch, he actually flops over for a nap in it. The stitching is solid and the buckles don't feel flimsy at all. My only real gripe is the neck section just slides over his head with no way to adjust it, so it's a tight squeeze past his ears every time. A quick snap or velcro opening there would've made a huge difference. Still, once it's on, it stays put and doesn't rub, so I'm happy enough to keep using it.
Re-watched the whole series twice now, and season three still holds up strong without any skips.
Arrived super fast, well packed, and feels really solid right out of the box.

This socks are simply amazing. So soft and breathable. They have a left and right side but I'm not sure it matters. They are pretty tall and come up onto my calves. I find myself wearing them all the time and I have a nice pair of nike flip flops that used to be the most comfortable thing I would wear around the house. Yes you can buy a pack of nike regular socks for almost the same price but they aren't anywhere near as comfortable as these. I'm thinking about switching all my socks out for these.

I was traveling and wanted something compelling, and even though I usually agree with the negative reviews of best sellers, I figured, How bad can it be? I read about 20 boring, annoying pages over the course of four days, and then picked up an Agatha Christie, which took me a day to finish. I just don't think this was well-written, and I don't mean the translation. I mean the narrative. Fiction 101 tells a writer to SHOW, NOT TELL. Instead of making there be anything vivid, let alone convincing, about these people or the pathos of the situation, Larsson tells tells tells. Additionally, as many have commented, Larsson spends so much time in exposition, there is no forward momentum. As stated, I couldn't go beyond 20 pages.

This really was a great find for a girl who loves Barbie. The accessories alone that are included are well worth the price and we had no issues with the mechanics of the vending machine. Would buy again definitely!

I strap these on a tree near my house and do dips, muscle ups, pull ups, push ups. Solid, sturdy design! I'm 220 lbs and feel confident these rings & straps won't snap! More worried about the tree branch! I drew an arrow on the buckle. So, I don't forget which way to stick the strap in.

I'll admit I wasn't expecting much at this price, but the padding is thicker than I figured and the zippers don't feel flimsy at all. It swallows my mirrorless setup with a couple lenses and still has space for snacks. Stitching isn't perfect, but for everyday hauling it's holding up way better than expected.

Had these mounted on my workshop doors for about six months now, through rain, snow, and summer heat. They still fire up instantly and haven't dimmed a bit. Housing feels solid and hasn't warped or yellowed. The motion detection is consistent, and they really light up the whole entrance. Impressed they've held up so well.
For the money, this case feels way more premium than I expected. Solid fit and clean look.

I picked these up on a whim after losing a pricey pair, and honestly I'm shocked at how secure the screw-on backs are. I've worn them through workouts, long workdays, and even slept in them a few times, zero slipping, no stabbing in the neck. The front-and-back design means I never have to fumble around figuring out which side faces out, which is a small thing that makes a big difference in the morning rush. The shine holds up well too; after weeks of daily wear they still look crisp and convincing. If you're tired of flimsy friction backs that disappear into the void, these are a solid fix.
Book arrived fast and in perfect shape, no dings or bends. The cover looks sharp and feels great in hand. Took a bit to get going, but once it did I was hooked. Solid read overall.

I wanted to like this more than I did. The setup has promise, city meets country, family tension, some sharp little moments, but it never really finds its footing. The pacing drags in the middle, and a few scenes feel like they're circling the same conflict without adding much. Performances are decent, especially the mom, who nails that passive-aggressive Southern charm, but some of the other characters feel underwritten and stuck in one note. I kept waiting for a bigger payoff or a deeper dive into the dynamics, but it stays pretty surface-level. Not a bad watch if you're in the mood for mild family drama, just don't expect much bite. Solid three stars, fine, but forgettable.

For the price, it's an absolute steal. I've already dog-eared half the pages just to revisit certain passages. Honestly feels like a book that'll stay on my nightstand for years. Rare to find this kind of depth without breaking the bank.
Did a 'kayamping' trip, took this along and had privacy for side of the river stops. It folds up great and flat, stored out of the way.

First thing I noticed pulling it out was how solid the white finish looked, no weird streaks or thin spots. It's got some heft to it but was super easy to slide over my standard 4x4. The World's End is a well-written action, middle-age life crisis, dark humored meets sci-fi film. The actors bring characters to life, making them believable. Like life, people aren't always what they seem; it's during crises that one's mettle is tested and who we are as human beings really revealed. The movie isn't exactly what I thought it was going to be about, but was NOT disappointing in even a small dose! My teenagers caught some of the little "pieces" that relate to Simon Pegg's & Nick Frost's two previous films ("Shaun of the Dead" and "Hot Fuzz"), which sometimes only consisted of, say, a piece of wrapper. The Special Features were enjoyable to watch. Seeing the actors not in character as they talked about the writing of the film, what their vision was, and how it came about was fun. I'll not write a summary of the movie-- you can get that anywhere. But the summary of our family's view of this film: We loved it! If you like British humor, if you liked Pegg's other two movies, then you'll likely enjoy this film, as well. Very well written, interesting characters, nice approach to the mixed-genre subjects.
For the price, it's a no-brainer, does exactly what I need without overcomplicating things.

I started looking for climbing toys for our now 17mth old before Christmas & after hours online, this was my fav. I planned to buy it for our daughters big Santa gift but hesitated after reading some reviews about poor safety features...when the priced dropped after Christmas, I'd still not found anything I liked better so I decided to give it a shot. I am SO glad I ignored the few negative reviews. It is absolutely perfect for our energetic, climbing toddler. She is able to climb the wall with no trouble, the slide is fast & long enough for her to enjoy, she can get a ball into the basketball hoop herself & while the potential is there with most any toy for a child to use it incorrectly and possibly get hurt, under minimal supervision, I see no imminent risk in this toy. (the concerns I read were toddlers "diving" through the hole for footballs to be thrown through). That particular opening in question does not hit our child in a place height wise where that is an issue. If she's standing, the hole is around her belly, if she sits, she can peek through the hole, but with the majority of her body safely on the platform, she isn't likely to fall from it. A child, if intentionally trying to dive through, coud get it through it I guess but it doesnt seem very probable. Again, I'm glad I bypassed that info and ordered anyway. I was also glad it was not quite as huge as I'd envisioned, it will be easy to move outside in warmer weather. It was also easy to put together which is a major plus! As a child development professional, I'd also recommend this for building gross motor skills. Definitely recommend this toy, we are so pleased with it...most importantly, so is our child! Alas, she has something she is allowed to climb on & it satisfies that natural interest for her, safely!

Watching a friend's vacation slides may be an agreeable way to spend an evening, but Laurie R. King tests readers' patience with the same technique when she sends Mary Russell and her husband, Sherlock Holmes, to "The Moor." The title refers to Dartmoor, nearly 400 square miles of fields, hills and treacherous marshes in the southwest of England. Conan Doyle set "The Hound of the Baskervilles" there, and King uses a rumored reappearance of the hound as the pretext for drawing her sleuthing couple there. They're summoned by the eccentric clergyman, author and hymn writer ("Onward Christian Soldiers") Sabine Baring-Gould, now nearly 90 and in failing health. But before the inquiry gets underway, we're given a long dissertation from Mrs. Holmes about Baring-Gould and Dartmoor. Between the suspicious death of a local who wandered off the straight and familiar and the discovery of a body in the good minister's lake, there's nearly 200 pages of Russell reading Baring-Gould's books, Russell talking with the man and Russell riding about the countryside and chatting up the locals. Getting to the heart of the hound mystery takes up the last hundred pages, with remarkably little of the suspense which the novel advertises.

I wasn't expecting much since I've read a ton of future-focused books, but this one actually grounds everything in everyday moments in a way that just clicks. Super relevant, a little unsettling, but impossible to put down.

It hasn't worn out or lost grip, which is good, but I sometimes notice it against my skin when I'm sitting between rounds. Overall, solid shorts that can take a beating. And double your reading fun when Taylor, the less glamourous twin to her sister Sheila, butts heads with Max, who becomes her live-in protector after mis-printing her personal ad to say that she is looking for a "sadomasochistic male." Taylor and Max have great chemistry and the story is sweet, funny and a fabulous read. I highly recommend it!

The wireless connection to the speed sensor kept dropping, so half my mileage didn't even get recorded. I tried fresh batteries and repositioning everything, but no luck. Honestly, my old basic wired unit from a decade ago was more reliable. For the price, I expected at least consistent basic functionality, not a gamble every time I head out. This Oxford Classics edition has much to recommend it. First, it is translated into smooth modern English, which, while not conveying every subtlety of Greek, will nontheless help the present-day reader through this long work. Second, the price is under $10. Third, there is an excellent map collection at the back showing the ancient world as we see it and as Herodotus saw it. The notes, bibliography, appendices, indexes, timeline and glossary all make this a great resource for the student or casual reader. There is enough mention of content in all the other reviews. I just wanted to point out the advantages of this edition.

I grabbed this on a whim because I remembered bits and pieces from late-night TV as a kid, but I wasn't sure if it would hold up. Honestly, it blew my expectations out of the water. The restoration is surprisingly crisp for a film this old, and the monster effects have this tangible, handcrafted charm you just don't get with modern CGI. I'd only ever seen the American cut before, so I wasn't prepared for how much tighter and more atmospheric the original version feels. The pacing is solid, the miniatures are wildly detailed, and there's a genuine sense of dread in the quieter scenes. It's become one of my go-to comfort watches, and I've already forced three friends to sit through it with me.

I laughed way more than I expected given the body count. The pacing is perfect, tight editing, zero filler. Honestly one of the most purely entertaining flicks I've watched this year.

Calvin Fisher left his Amish community at the tender age of fourteen after having a problematic relationship with his family. Calvin only returned to Hart County because his beloved brother, Mark has fallen ill. As Calvin works to make amends for his troubled past and questionable job, he crosses path with a sheltered local nursery school teacher, Alice Yoder. Alice is wary of Calvin because of his questionable past, but she can't help feeling an attraction to him. Although Calvin assures Alice that he is worthy of her friendship, she can't help to feel conflicted. Will Calvin be able to tell his family why he is working in a gang? Will Alice be able to trust Calvin only has her best interest at heart? I have read a few of Gray's books and loved each novel because each one had a surprise element. However, I felt like this novel fell short and lacked excitement. The person who causing all of the trouble was very obvious even though there were two people of interest. I did like how Gray humanized and showed each characters' struggling between doing what is right and following their hearts. Fans of Gray's work would be happy to know this novel has recurring characters.

I'm a 36B, which means that I don't need any heavy-duty compression with exercise (I'm a light jogger), and this bra is labeled as a "medium support bra," which I was fine with--I was just hoping to avoid the typical sports bra uni-boob. I'd read the negative reviews from others who brought this bra, in which they noted that the bra didn't provide enough support--which made sense to me, once I saw the fabric in the bra cups, which consists of a fine and breathable mesh material...and my guess is that it wouldn't do such a great job of keeping your nipples from showing through a t-shirt. Anyway, I do think the bra is well-made, and this company is known for it's quality products, but here's my major complaint and advice to you regarding this bra: if you're a size C cup or smaller, don't bother (or those with C cups should consider the B cup), because the 36B bra that I purchased had huge, loose, silly cups...I put the thing on, and I started laughing at myself in the mirror. It was obvious that, in order for me to fill the bra cups, I needed to almost triple my cup size. Seriously. It was hilarious. I'd say that the only people who would benefit from this bra are those busty ladies looking for a basic, mildly supportive sports bra for the type of exercise that yields little-to-no boob bouncing--like on an elliptical machine, or slow-paced walking. Or perhaps for those busty ladies who don't exercise at all, but just need a bit of lifting and separating... I'm open to suggestions from anyone who actually wears a 36B (nothing bigger--there are plenty of bra reviews out there from those with larger cup sizes that I can refer to) and can advise on a sports bra that's less than $50, does not lead to uni-boob, and actually provides at least medium support. I also think it would be great if Amazon came up with an option to search and read bra reviews based on your actual size...

I was hoping for a fast, immersive fantasy adventure based on the sample and the buzz, but this turned into a slog. The author packs every paragraph with so much overwrought description that it chokes any sense of momentum. A character can't simply walk across a room, we get a full inventory of the floorboards, the quality of light, and probably their childhood memories of carpentry. I'm all for rich world-building, but here it feels like padding disguised as style. The story underneath seems promising, which makes it more frustrating, because I kept losing the thread in all the verbal foliage. Ended up skimming long stretches just to find dialogue or something actually happening. Not what I signed up for.

I've been using this for a few months now and it's held up perfectly, no flickering or loose connections at all. Solid little adapter.
Love it! Good quality and nice soft night light and noise variations
Had this thing for months now and it's taken a beating but still runs like new. Kids love it.
Love how these fit and they match everything with a pair of jeans.

I wanted to like this more, especially since I grabbed it during a Kindle deal for under five bucks. For that price, it's decent entertainment if you're just looking to kill a few hours, but the story itself felt relentless in a way that didn't always pay off. The pacing is solid and Barr's nature writing is still sharp, so you get some atmosphere for your money. Where it falls short is balance, it's so grim and stripped-down that I never felt that spark I used to get from this series. If you're a completist or curious at a discount, it's okay value. Just don't expect the layered storytelling from the earlier books.
As usual, Hogue products are the best! Very tight fit, so using a little windex to moisten helps rock it on a lot easier.
Only worth buying if you love wasting money and time.
Fantastic way to hide litter boxes. Looks great and easy to assemble. Also easy to clean. Cat approved.
Bass is surprisingly deep for such a tiny speaker.

I grabbed this hoping it would just look decent, but it honestly blew my expectations away. The texture feels way nicer than I anticipated, and it somehow makes our tiny breakfast corner look intentionally styled. Hides stray crumbs like a champ too. So glad I took a chance on it.
I've had these for a few months and they're holding up fine, but the fabric started pilling a bit sooner than I expected.

I've had this right angle clamp for about six months now, mainly using it for welding small brackets and frames in my garage. The aluminum jaws have held up surprisingly well, no warping or gouging even after repeated exposure to heat and spatter. The steel threads still turn smoothly without binding, and I haven't noticed any slop developing in the floating center piece. I did knock a star because the powder coat started wearing thin on the inside edges after a few heavy sessions, but it's purely cosmetic. It's proven itself tough enough for regular hobby work, and I'd buy another if this one ever gives out.
My kids now beg to watch this every Friday night, the music alone is worth it.

I expected more emotional depth, but the pacing felt rushed and the main character's realization came too easily. It's a sweet idea, just didn't hit as hard as I'd hoped. Decent for a quick, light read, but I probably won't revisit it.
I honestly expected these to just look nice on the shelf, but they feel super solid in hand and pour cleanly every time. The vibrant finish hasn't dulled after weeks of daily use.

I've wanted this minifigure for a while, didn't get it because it was a bit expensive. I have to say I'm a bit disappointed. The colors were not as seen in the pictures. It looks more yellowish that orangish. Which makes it look cheap and more like a knock off. I tried to fit it in the Lego minifigue dispay case, but it was pretty hard to fit since there is a hose coming from his backpack tank, and the front one is long too. In conclusion I like this guy, but not a big fan of the color the colors. If I could I would paint it.

One of my favorite baseball movies ever, and (if Wikipedia can be believed) closer to real life than many other 'based on a true story' flicks. Inspirational, with a solid performance from Quaid and the supporting cast also does a great job. Would rent it again, and recommend watching with younger kids who still think that baseball and their Dads are great.

Really enjoyed this book. It was awesome. Two people who lost and found each other. They had their troubles but could not overcome their feelings. So glad they found each other. They fit. Loved and lost but to only find love again
Great Bluetooth mouse. Never thought it would make much sense because I've had bad experiences with BT mice. Great Microsoft.

I needed something powerful for my workshop bench and this thing delivers. Light output is intense and clean, no weird color cast. Perfect for anyone doing detail work or in a garage. My electrician buddy already ordered one after seeing it.

For four bucks a pop, I didn't expect anything fancy, and these do the job, solid fit, no wobble. The satin nickel is definitely on the muted side, almost flat in certain light, which bums me out a little. They feel sturdy enough for a guest bath or hallway closet, just don't expect any visual pop. Decent buy if you're on a tight budget and can live with a super understated look.
Love the lids for the microwave. They prevent splatter and help keep it clean. Easy to wash. Love the hanging hole - I have them hanging on the inside of a cabinet door.
My toddler loves the vibrant colors, and the chalk washes off the driveway with zero scrubbing. A quick hose-down and it's gone, huge relief after a long afternoon of messy, creative fun.

Best thing about this glove is the material.. it has a stretchy feel to it and it breaths great. really helps on hot days when you can feel your hands sweat, always gave me a confident grip. Would buy again but I wish it had a ball marker on the cuff.

Wasn't sure how these would hold up over time, but after six months they still feel solid. Used them on a gate that gets slammed daily and no loosening or rust yet. Only minor gripe is one screw felt a tiny bit soft during install.
Love the shoe. Alittle tight but I think they will loosen as I wear them
Good quality. Easy to apply n sticks really well to the wall. Tip: Clean wall with alcohol prior to apply n let it dry before applying it
I keep my study Bible in this, and the padded cover makes carrying it to small group feel secure. The handle is surprisingly sturdy, and it fits my highlighters too.

Took this on a day hike and it held up nicely. The padding kept my gear secure even when I stumbled on rocky terrain. I managed to fit my mirrorless body with a prime lens, two batteries, and a few small accessories. Just be realistic about the interior space before loading it up. Solid little bag overall.
Arrived super fast in sturdy packaging. Opened the bag and my pup's tail started going crazy before I even gave her one.
I got this hoping it would hold up, but the metal dents way too easily. The engraving is super faint, so I can barely read my dog's info now. Disappointing for the price.

Way better than expected, still our go-to ball after months of use.

Got this for under ten bucks and it was totally worth it. The firsthand look into the belief system and daily pressures was eye-opening. Some parts felt a bit rushed, but the emotional weight of leaving everything behind at such a young age really hit me. Solid read for the price.
Came packed super tight but the box was a bit banged up.
Glass is beautiful, but the wire connector feels really flimsy.
Looks great, zero effort, brightens up the room.

I've had these for a few months now and they're holding up surprisingly well, scuffs wipe off easily and the canvas hasn't frayed at all. The stitching around the toe cap is still solid too, which I was a little worried about at first. I wear them a couple times a week, mostly for casual errands, and they've stayed bright without fading. Cushioning is minimal as expected, so I wouldn't pick them for long walks, but for everyday wear they've been great.

Only 3 bones in this small bag maybe to keep the costs down I am not sure. I gave one to my dog he put it in his mouth and dropped it and went and sat on another side of the room. So I brought it to him when he was sitting down and gave it to him and he suprisingly ate it but ate it too quickly and threw half of it up. That was enough for me and I threw the rest of them in the garbage. Too bad I have tried Rachael Ray dog food in the past and my dog loved that but this was a flop.

I was a little hesitant to purchase glasses online, however these arrived perfectly wrapped! Don't let the photos fool you, these glasses are a sturdy set, feel great to hold with a medium weight and hold a lot. I am currently using them for water, or iced tea but really any beverage would work. I am very pleased with the quality. Although they are a bit large, they wash superbly in the dishwasher and store nicely. Even my husband who can't get excited about glasses loved them. We have them displayed in our cabinets behind glass and they look great. I think the value surpasses the price. I am extremely pleased with my purchase and will buy from Amazon and the Libbey brand again in the future. Thank you Amazon for free shipping!
Works just like the stock GoPro battery if not better. Fast charger. Two additional batteries are more that enough for any trip.
My son wanted a red Yoshi and this fit the bill. Not the best quality, comparable to a carnival prize stuffed toy.

Low cost insurance to save your life, carbon monoxide is colorless, tasteless, and oderless, take it from someone who learned 35 years ago the hard way. perfect to use when I'm on my dailey execrise program.

My grandson loves this stuffed dog. It is so soft, and he sleeps with it every night. I purchased two of these so that one stays at his house, and one is available for sleepovers at our house.

I actually bought this to use with small jar candles rather than tarts. It works perfectly for me and I love the pretty green color. I cannot have an open flame in my office, so this is an excellent substitute. It heats the small candle thoroughly, does not take up lot of room, and is easy to use. The cord could be longer, but this is not a deal breaker. I love the product itself and am very happy I decided to get it. My office smells wonderful all the time now!

My copy arrived in a snug cardboard wrap with zero dings, crisp dust jacket, vibrant colors, no bent corners. Flipping it open felt like unwrapping a childhood memory. The pages are thick, the binding solid, and that familiar playful cover instantly brought a grin to my face before I even read a single poem.

My copy arrived two days early in a sturdy cardboard mailer, no bends or scuffs at all. The cover art looked even richer in person, with a slight matte finish that feels great to hold. I cracked it open just to skim the first page and ended up reading the whole first chapter standing in my kitchen. The paper quality is smooth and the print is crisp, which made diving into Patricia's impulsive journey so easy on the eyes. You can tell the publisher cared about the physical experience. Honestly, from the satisfying weight of the book to how fresh it smelled straight out of the package, the unboxing alone made me excited to keep reading.

This is the second time it broke in the same area, the part where you push to close door and lock it. They gave me a new one. I have only had this a year with light use and it broke again. Super annoyed, do not waste your money.

I am very happy with these churidars. They not only look nice, but can accommodate my body which is not small. I bought others in a large size and they were incredibly small and a waste of money and time. These particular churidars should accommodate various body sizes.

I like the durability of these, I am not sold on their oblong size. I bought these because I was under the impression that they fit in spice jars. They don't fit as well as I'd hoped. You need to do some manipulating which causes some of the measurement to fall out.

I just set this up today, so I don't have a long-term review for this but here's what I like about it so far. It's very attractive (shocking for a pet fountain), because it's ceramic rather than plastic. So much better looking than the plastic ones I've had before. It looks like a decor piece instead of pet fountain, which makes me feel less like a crazy cat lady. And Emily, my cat, has already approved it. I'm keeping my fingers crossed and hope it will last a long time.
Planet Dog................need I say more? Made in the USA, safe for your pet, if a dog doesn't like Planet Dog toys, they won't like any toys! My three are sold on Planet Dog!

Great new book in the Suitors of Seattle series!! Hyacinth has always felt like the odd duck in her family of beautiful flowers. She loves to write and to read and spends most of her time at siher ster's library working on her book. Her sisters friend Lawrence thinks he is in love with her and hopes to win her over by following the advice of a newspaper column. Loved this from start to finish!!!!

I love how simple and straight forward these smoothies are to make. I can see myself definitely trying a lot of these smoothie! It matters not if your a die hard Paleo purist or a regular health foodie, you're sure to enjoy these smoothies!
Thought the tough nylon meant long-lasting, but my pup sniffed it once and walked away. Total waste.

It will probably work OK on powder burning rifles, but does not work on air rifles. Save your money on this model, and drop some more for the V3 version. Night and day difference between the two models. V3 more money, but more sensitive, has a SD card, more mounting options, on and on.

Bought this to simplify our home backup routine, and it's been smooth overall. The transfer speeds are solid for regular file dumps, and setup was basically plug-and-play. My only minor gripe is the proprietary cable feels a bit delicate where it plugs into the drive. I just handle it gently and it's been fine for a few months now. Great value for straightforward storage.

I wanted to love this more than I did. The concept is fun and the first half really pulled me in, Myrna's job is genuinely clever and the dragon personalities are a highlight. But after a few months sitting on my Kindle, trying to revisit it, the cracks started showing. The middle section drags hard, and the will-they-won't-they with the ex gets repetitive instead of tense. It held up okay as a light read initially, but on a second pass the humor felt thinner and the plot lost its snap. Not bad by any means, just not something that sticks with you or rewards a re-read. Fine for a single breezy afternoon, but the shine wore off quicker than I expected.
Here is a good to great documentary on the Roman Empire. Part of me wishes I lived back then. But I would have been a slave not a free man as I am today.

I have the biggest smile on my face after reading this book; it's the stuff your dream romance is made of. It's honest, mature, sexy, relevant, engaging and most importantly its romantic; swoon worthy. I started this book ready to resist liking Arden. While she is still not my favorite character in this series, the author did a phenomenal job making her softer and vulnerable while staying true to her "real' personality. I certainly understood her better by the end of the book and look forward to the author unveiling the rest of her complicated layers. I think the good doctor is just what she needed in more ways than one. The author gives us a great chance to catch up with all of your favorite couples from the previous two stories; Ari/Riely, Athena/Denali and Daynesha/Cesar. What I really enjoyed and respected the most was the way these couples dealt with conflict when it arose, textbook for classy and mature adults. This book is as much about this cast of friends as it is learning more about Arden and what she needed most in her life. You simply cannot read this book and not wish you were part of this group's inner circle. My favorite line in the book is delivered by my favorite man in this series, Cesar, he tells Athena "You are the diamond Dee has been longing to treasure". This book is well written with its depth and characterization. The drama is not overdone (thank you) and the pace is perfect. I feel as though I know this group of friends. The men of this series are the type of men that every girl dreams of one day making their very own. Well done. I do have a question......Who is up next?
A fun, fast read for anyone who loves a spicy enemies-to-lovers vibe. Definitely recommend for a weekend escape.
These glasses are the answer - the right price and the right, light, comfortable fit.

I wanted a good sturdy slip cover to protect my chairs from kids. These do that job well. They get dirty, but they are easy to take on and off and wash up in the washing machine really well. I'd buy them again.
The loud alarm and flashing lights on this surge protector give me real peace of mind during storms.
Best tasting trail food we have ever tried, my wife wants to quit cooking and just boil water!!
Put these up months ago, still solid as ever.

This thing was a total waste of time. The pacing dragged so badly I almost turned it off twice. Action scenes felt cheap and pointless. Honestly, I'd rather rewatch paint dry than sit through this again.

I've watched this probably six times now over the last couple years, and it honestly holds up in ways I didn't expect. The disc itself still plays flawlessly, no skips, no freezing, even after my toddler got his sticky hands on the case. The emotional core doesn't wear thin with repeat viewings, either. Some scenes hit harder the second or third time around once you know where things are headed. I docked one star because the menu navigation is a little clunky and feels dated, but the movie itself is solid as a rock. It's become one of those quiet, dependable comfort watches I keep coming back to when life gets messy. Definitely built to last, both technically and emotionally.
This story caught me off guard in the best way, genuinely uplifting without ever feeling forced. Finished it in one sitting and immediately wanted more.

For the price, these little files are a solid addition to my tinkering kit. I've been using them to clean up 3D prints and deburr small metal brackets, and they handle light-duty work surprisingly well. The grip is comfortable enough, and the cutting surfaces haven't dulled after a few projects. You might wish for a wider variety of shapes, but honestly, at this cost, I can't complain much. They're handy to have around.

I wanted to love this Nostalgia gadget as much as the old electric peeler I had years ago, but it falls a bit short. The spiral cutting works decently for zucchini noodles, and the shredder handles a block of cheese without much fuss. The peeling function, though, is where it loses me. I expected it to glide through russet potatoes like my previous unit, but it struggles on anything with deep eyes or uneven shape, lots of stopping to reposition. Cleanup is okay, but food gets trapped around the blade housing. For the price, it's fine as a basic shredder and spiralizer, just don't expect it to replace a dedicated peeler.
Exactly the quality I hoped for, rugged, comfortable right out of the box, and true to size.

I got this for my teenage nephew who's pretty shy. The sections on making conversation are practical and easy to digest. Some advice felt a little dated, but overall it sparked a few good chats between us. Solid pick for a kid needing a gentle nudge.
Showed up fast, packed solid, and feels super capable right away.

I grabbed this amp to replace a bulky receiver in my workshop setup, and I'm genuinely surprised by how clean it sounds. The Bluetooth pairing was dead simple, connected to my phone on the first try and hasn't dropped once, even from across the room. I'm driving a pair of old bookshelf speakers, and the high-end detail is crisp without being harsh. What really got me was the subwoofer output; I plugged in a small powered sub and suddenly my workbench felt like a mini listening lounge. It runs cool even after hours of use, and the compact size tucks neatly under a shelf. For the price, the connectivity alone makes it a steal.

The Easter bunny brought this for my 4 year old daughter! She LOVES IT!!! She totally thinks it's the real deal. The items are sturdy, far beyond dollar store play makeup plastic. The perfect gift for my daughter.

It was OK, less impressive than Lamb or Fool by C Moore. Slow start and not as funny as his other works. Had bits of charm and wit that redeemed it though not enough for me to recommend spending money on it. Worth reading if borrowed from a friend.

The fabric also faded way faster than I expected, and there's now a weird thin spot near the knee that looks like it'll tear soon. They're not completely unwearable yet, but for something I've only had a couple months, the durability is disappointing. Definitely won't hold up for heavy use.

I went into this one with pretty low expectations, mostly because I'd only seen a couple of the movie adaptations and figured the books would feel overly sentimental in print. I was wrong. The writing is much more grounded than I expected, and the characters actually have some depth beyond the romance. It's not literary fiction by any stretch, but the pacing is spot-on and I finished it in just a few sittings. The emotional beats hit harder than I thought they would, and a couple scenes genuinely got me. I'm knocking off one star because the ending felt a little rushed and tidy compared to the buildup, but overall it exceeded what I assumed a mass-market love story would deliver. I'll definitely pick up another one.

I honestly wasn't expecting much when I unboxed this thing, but from the first song I was totally hooked. The clarity is wild, vocals come through so clean and the low end has a surprising punch for its size. I've taken it to small backyard hangouts and even used it during a presentation in a decent-sized room; it filled the space without breaking a sweat. Pairing was instant and the battery lasted way longer than I anticipated. It's become my go-to grab-and-go speaker for just about everything. If you're on the fence, just get it, you'll be grinning ear to ear the moment you hit play.

For the price, I expected way better durability and stability. Ended up tossing it in the back of a cabinet.

My book arrived with the spine already cracked and the cover bent backward like someone had used it as a doorstop. It was just tossed in a thin bubble mailer with zero padding, so the corners are all mashed and the dust jacket has a big ugly tear down the front. For a brand new hardcover, it honestly looks like a used library reject. I didn't even want to touch it after seeing the greasy smudge across the first few pages. Total letdown before I even got to chapter one. Really kills any excitement about the story when the thing shows up looking this shabby.
I have read all the Dragonfury Series and am anxiously awaiting Coreenes next one. I have enjoyed all her books and recommend you read all of them.
Thought I'd get a standard biography, but it's a thoughtful blend of history and travel. A bit long-winded, yet surprisingly engaging.
Not quite the masterclass I expected, but still solid and enjoyable.

I've had this book for a few weeks now and keep coming back to reread passages, it holds up so well. The emotional depth doesn't fade with time; each revisit reveals more layers in Dream's journey and the bonds between these women. It's not just a one-time read, it sticks with you and grows richer. The storytelling stays solid even after you know the plot twists, which is rare. Really impressed by how well this story wears over time.
Only thing I can say is that I feel like it's perfect for what I wanted, and easily worth the money
My oldest one's been dropped on rock, dirt, and concrete more times than I can count. Still seals tight and doesn't leak a drop after three years.

I've tried substituting in many of my baking recipes with some good and some bad results. I carefully inspected each recipe, expecting to find problems, but couldn't - they are all very achievable. I'm interested in trying the raw desserts and the brownies that use beans. Who can resist brownies?! This book is formatted well and I appreciate that calories and nutritional information is included for each.

I really wanted to love this updated version because the banking unit is super handy and keeps things moving without piles of paper money everywhere. But the action cards just don't hit the same as the old Life tiles. My kids got bored fast when half the cards were stuff like "tell a joke" or "do five jumping jacks" instead of actual life events. It felt more like a party game than the classic board game I remembered. The electronic spinner is a nice touch and saves arguments, but the whole vibe is just off. We'll probably keep it for quick games, but I'm already hunting down a vintage set instead.
The screws that came with them do not fit my 12-24 drilled and tap rack fastener holes. The shelves are nice but I had to go buy 12-24 screws from a hardware store in order to install them.

My 9yr old has worn them three times skiing. Temp highs were -2, 2, and 34F. They work in the cold, and he isn't complaining about his feet being cold. For a little more you can buy a tougher sock, but my son would out grow them.
I wish these jeans would come back. I have large thighs and these were nice and roomy in the thigh.

I picked this up on a whim and ended up losing a whole weekend to it. The Inspector Banks series is new to me, but this one grabbed me right from the start. There's a really lived-in feel to the Yorkshire setting, and the way the investigation unfolds feels patient and believable, no cheap twists, just solid police work and flawed, human characters. I loved how the victim's messy life slowly comes into focus, and the small-town dynamics add a layer of tension you don't always get in bigger city procedurals. Robinson's writing is crisp and never showy, but the atmosphere sticks with you. Already ordered the next three books in the series.

I couldn't put this book down once I got into the first few chapters. The way Sanderson builds the Reckoners' guerrilla tactics against seemingly invincible Epics felt so grounded and clever, especially the sequence where they plan a hit using detailed research on a target's specific weakness. That level of tactical problem-solving hooked me more than any flashy power display could. It's smart, tense, and refreshingly different from typical cape stories.

I love Gentle Leaders- well worth money to keep your dog from pulling you down the street. To personalize ours, I sew bows on the nose area- not blocking dog's vision, but off to side- so everyone knows she is not only friendly, but a female as our medium sized dog is a lab/beagle mix- if you never used one, get it and be sure to READ INSTRUCTIONS on how to adjust and use!
this is a nice windbreaker jacket for cool weather. it is thin and lightweight. I'm not used to this type of collar but that's not a big deal. nice fit.
I put this on a sandwich, it tastes terrible. Looks like mayo, but it's not. Do not eat this. Works great for sealing leaks though.
Very nice product. Was very easy to add strip LED lighting and create professional looking enclosed LED shelf lighting. The angle helps direct the light in and it looks great.
After a season of rough scrambles it still looks new and holds up way better than the original padding.
My daughter loves how the tail rattles when you shake it. The plush fabric stayed soft even after a wash.

I went with this because the ingredient list looked solid for the price, and it mostly lives up to that. It's denser than I expected, way less water than the pate styles my two picky eaters are used to, so you definitely get your money's worth on actual meat content. That said, the texture threw them off at first. I have to mash it with a fork and mix in a splash of warm water before they'll even approach the bowl. Once I do that, they clean their plates. Slight extra effort, but I'm okay with it given the quality. If your cats are used to soupy food, just know you'll need to do a little prep.

I've used a number of other bluetooth headphones, and these have had the best sound and comfort by far. My ONLY complaint is that they're not waterproofed, so using them while working out can lead to bad things when sweat gets into them. Aside from that, I've yet to find a pair of bluetooth headphones that match them.
I wanted to like this infuser, but for the money it's just not worth it. The mesh lets all the fine bits through, so I end up with a gritty, undrinkable cup every time.

I've had this reel for a few months now and it's been solid overall. The drag is consistent and holds up fine on decent-sized fish. It's not the fanciest thing out there, but it feels sturdy and balances well on my 7-foot rod. A little tweaking was needed after a few trips, but nothing major. For the price, I'm happy with how it performs.

Leather & Lace: Inferno Glory MC #1 by J.A. Fredericks is the first book in the Inferno Glory MC four-part serial. It is the story of Harley Sonnick and Colt Sawyer. It is NOT a rosy and warm story full of happy feel-good moments. It is a story filled with mistakes, regrets, deceit, danger, and betrayal. We meet Harley when she is fresh out of prison. She just served three years for a crime she was set up for and unknowingly committed. She lost her dad while she was locked up, his battle with lung cancer finally over. She goes to the only place she has ever called home, the Inferno Glory MC clubhouse. When she gets there, the man who has always been another father figure to her turns his back on her. This is when she meets Colt. With no place to go she begrudgingly takes him up on his offer to crash at his place for a short while. It doesn't hurt that he is OFF THE CHARTS HAWT!!!! Colt is instantly enamored with the vixen on two wheels and jumps at the chance to be her (paraphrasing book) knight in a leather cut riding in on his black hog to save her. The love/lust is kinda insta-lust that grows into some deep feelings fast and the sex is raunchy and will melt your undies right-off. Despite the romantic aspect there is an undercurrent of subterfuge and suspense and the book ends on a cliffhanger in anticipation of the next book, Pleasure & Pain: Inferno Glory MC #2. I thoroughly enjoyed this quick read and can't wait to see what's going to happen next!
My betta doesn't use it, but I don't think that it is a reflection on the quality of the product. It is bendable and nice looking. Serves its purpose.
The wood grain detail is a nice touch, but it shows dust way too easily.
Still going strong after a dozen trips, this thing takes a beating and keeps coffee hot.

Any regular reader of John Sandford will not be disappointed. This is the usual mix of an over the top crime, a cast of funky good guys, and irreverent dialogue. I have always found Sandford's books so well written. He is a craftsman of this genre. I enthusiastically recommend this read to the usual fans and to anyone new to this author.

Rx Missing (Decorah Security Series, Book 10) Absolutely love Rebecca York's books! Decorah Security is one of my favorites. The characters are well defined. You feel like rooting for them to solve the mystery and get on with their life. Hope there is a story for Grant.

Received this flashlight just before hurricane Matthew hit Florida. Love it!! Came with batteries installed so we could use right away.
